items.title: Padma Naresh Vardhineedi: Revolutionizing Financial Planning Through Cloud Innovation
items.author: Kashvi Pandey
items.content:
\ In an era where financial technology demands unprecedented levels of security, scalability, and efficiency, Padma Naresh Vardhineedi's groundbreaking work in financial planning solutions stands as a testament to architectural excellence and innovative leadership. Through his role as a distinguished software architect, Vardhineedi has transformed how financial advisors create and manage client financial plans, setting new standards for enterprise-grade cloud solutions in the finance sector. His visionary approach to cloud architecture and unwavering commitment to technical excellence has redefined what's possible in financial technology infrastructure.

\ At the heart of this achievement lies a sophisticated AWS-based architecture that redefined the possibilities of financial planning technology. Under Padma's leadership, the project emerged as a comprehensive solution that not only met but exceeded modern financial institutions' demanding requirements for security, performance, and reliability. The architecture's design demonstrated exceptional foresight, incorporating cutting-edge cloud services while maintaining the flexibility needed for future scaling and enhancement.

\ The implementation showcased Padma's exceptional ability to architect complex systems, particularly in his innovative approach to security architecture. By implementing Lambda authorizers at the API Gateway level, he introduced a revolutionary authentication mechanism that enhanced security while significantly improving system performance. This architectural decision led to a remarkable 30% reduction in processing overhead, demonstrating how thoughtful design can simultaneously enhance both security and efficiency. The implementation of Lambda authorizers proved particularly innovative, as it eliminated redundant authentication logic within microservices while maintaining robust security standards across the entire application ecosystem.

\ The project's technical sophistication extended deep into its data processing capabilities. Padma's expertise in data engineering and infrastructure automation proved transformative for the financial planning workflow. Through carefully designed AWS Glue jobs, he achieved a 40% reduction in overnight data processing time – a critical improvement that provided financial advisors with earlier access to updated client insights and market data. This optimization fundamentally changed how advisors could serve their clients, enabling more responsive and data-driven financial planning services. The data processing pipeline's design showcased particular innovation in its use of NoSQL databases, specifically Amazon DocumentDB and DynamoDB, which provided the perfect balance of flexibility and performance for handling complex financial data structures.

\ Infrastructure automation emerged as another cornerstone of the project's success. Through sophisticated infrastructure as code (IaC) implementation using Terraform, Padma established a framework for sustainable growth and cost optimization. This approach resulted in approximately 25% savings in cloud resource costs while simultaneously improving the platform's scalability and reliability. The Terraform scripts developed under his leadership became a model for infrastructure automation, demonstrating how modern DevOps practices can be effectively applied to financial technology solutions. The automation framework not only reduced operational overhead but also ensured consistent deployment across different environments, significantly reducing the risk of configuration-related issues.

\ The high-availability architecture designed by Padma showcased exceptional attention to fault tolerance and disaster recovery. By leveraging AWS's global infrastructure and implementing sophisticated failover mechanisms, the solution achieved remarkable uptime statistics while maintaining data integrity and security. The architecture's design particularly excelled in its approach to data replication and backup strategies, ensuring business continuity even in challenging scenarios.

\ Stakeholder impact was significant across multiple dimensions. Financial advisors gained access to a more efficient, reliable platform that accelerated their workflow, enabling them to serve more clients more effectively. Organizations benefited from reduced operational costs and improved scalability, while maintaining compliance with stringent financial industry regulations. Most importantly, end clients received better service through faster, more secure access to their financial planning data, with improved visualization and analysis capabilities enhancing their understanding of their financial position.

\ The project's success in integrating complex technologies while maintaining simplicity in operation demonstrated Padma's exceptional architectural vision. His approach to system design balanced immediate needs with future scalability, ensuring the solution could evolve alongside changing business requirements. The architecture's modular design particularly stood out, allowing for easy integration of new features and capabilities without disrupting existing functionality.

\ As a distinguished software architect and technology leader, Padma Naresh Vardhineedi continues to shape the future of digital transformation in the financial sector. His expertise in cloud computing, AI-driven software engineering, and enterprise architecture has proven instrumental in delivering solutions that not only meet current needs but anticipate future challenges. This project stands as a testament to his ability to leverage cutting-edge technology to create lasting business impact, while maintaining the highest standards of security and performance.

\ The security architecture designed by Padma deserves particular mention for its innovative approach to authentication and authorization. By implementing Lambda authorizers at the gateway level, he created a more efficient and maintainable security model that significantly reduced the complexity of service-level security implementations. This approach not only improved system performance but also enhanced the overall security posture of the application, making it more resistant to modern cyber threats.

\ Looking ahead, the implications of this architectural innovation extend far beyond the immediate project success. As financial institutions increasingly embrace digital transformation, the patterns and practices established by Padma serve as a blueprint for future implementations. His work demonstrates how thoughtful architecture and technical leadership can drive meaningful change in how financial services are delivered and managed. The scalability built into the system's design ensures it can accommodate growing data volumes and user bases while maintaining optimal performance.

\ The success of this financial planning solution project has further cemented Vardhineedi's position as a leading voice in software architecture and cloud innovation. His contributions to the field serve as a model for how technical excellence and business value can align to create transformative solutions. The project's success has influenced how financial institutions approach their digital transformation initiatives, setting new standards for what's possible in financial technology infrastructure.

About Padma Naresh Vardhineedi

\ Padma Naresh Vardhineedi is a distinguished software architect and technology leader whose expertise spans cloud computing, AI-driven software engineering, and enterprise architecture. With a proven track record in designing scalable, high-performance solutions across industries, he has consistently delivered innovations that drive digital transformation forward. His deep understanding of cloud technologies, particularly AWS services, combined with his expertise in security architecture and infrastructure automation, has enabled him to create solutions that set new standards for efficiency and reliability in enterprise software development.

\ In an industry that demands continuous innovation and unwavering security, Padma Vardhineedi stands out for his ability to balance technical excellence with practical business requirements. His approach to architecture emphasizes not only technical sophistication but also operational efficiency and user experience. As the technology landscape continues to evolve, his contributions to software architecture and cloud computing continue to influence how organizations approach their digital transformation journeys.

items.title: Madison Square Garden Bans Fan After Surveillance System IDs Him as Critic of Its CEO
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
An anonymous reader quotes a report from The Verge: A concert on Monday night at New York's Radio City Music Hall was a special occasion for Frank Miller: his parents' wedding anniversary. He didn't end up seeing the show -- and before he could even get past security, he was informed that he was in fact banned for life from the venue and all other properties owned by Madison Square Garden (MSG). After scanning his ticket and promptly being pulled aside by security, Miller was told by staff that he was barred from the MSG properties for an incident at the Garden in 2021. But Miller says he hasn't been to the venue in nearly two decades.
 
"They hand me a piece of paper letting me know that I've been added to a ban list," Miller says. "There's a trespass notice if I ever show up on any MSG property ever again," which includes venues like Radio City, the Beacon Theatre, the Sphere, and the Chicago Theatre. He was baffled at first. Then it dawned on him: this was probably about a T-shirt he designed years ago. MSG Entertainment won't say what happened with Miller or how he was picked out of the crowd, but he suspects he was identified via controversial facial recognition systems that the company deploys at its venues.
 
In 2017, 1990s New York Knicks star Charles Oakley was forcibly removed from his seat near Knicks owner and Madison Square Garden CEO James Dolan. The high-profile incident later spiraled into an ongoing legal battle. For Miller, Oakley was an "integral" part of the '90s Knicks, he says. With his background in graphic design, he made a shirt in the style of the old team logo that read, "Ban Dolan" -- a reference to the infamous scuffle. A few years later, in 2021, a friend of Miller's wore a Ban Dolan shirt to a Knicks game and was kicked out and banned from future events. That incident spawned ESPN segments and news articles and validated what many fans saw as a pettiness on Dolan and MSG's part for going after individual fans who criticized team ownership. "Frank Miller Jr. made threats against an MSG executive on social media and produced and sold merchandise that was offensive in nature," Mikyl Cordova, executive vice president of communications and marketing for the company, said in an emailed statement. "His behavior was disrespectful and disruptive and in violation of our code of conduct."
 
Miller responded to the ban, saying: "I just found it comical, until I was told that my mom was crying [in the lobby]. I was like, 'Oh man, I ruined their anniversary with my shit talk on the internet. Memes are powerful, and so is the surveillance state. It's something that we all have to be aware of -- the panopticon. We're [being] surveilled at all times, and it's always framed as a safety thing, when rarely is that the case. It's more of a deterrent and a fear tactic to try to keep people in line."

items.title: NonlinearSolve.jl: Other References You Can Turn to
items.author: Linearization Technology
items.content:
Solving systems of nonlinear equations is a fundamental challenge that arises across many scientific domains. This paper presented NonlinearSolve.jl, a high-performance and robust open-source solver for nonlinear systems implemented natively in the Julia programming language. Through extensive numerical experiments on benchmark problems, real-world applications, and scalability tests, we have demonstrated the superior capabilities of NonlinearSolve.jl compared to existing software tools.

\ Key strengths of NonlinearSolve.jl include its flexible unified API for rapidly experimenting with different solver options, smart automatic algorithm selection for balancing speed and robustness, specialized non-allocating kernels for small systems, automatic sparsity exploitation, and support for Jacobian-free Krylov methods. These features enable NonlinearSolve.jl to reliably solve challenging nonlinear problems, including cases where standard solvers fail while attaining high performance through techniques.

items.title: What is the Large Ill-Conditioned Nonlinear Brusselator System?
items.author: Linearization Technology
items.content:
\ and the initial conditions are

\ with the periodic boundary condition

\ We analyze our solvers for this problem in 3 parts. Firstly, Figure 10 compares the scaling of NewtonRaphson and TrustRegion between frameworks. NonlinearSolve.jl leverages sparsity detection and colored sparse matrix algorithms to accelerate Jacobian construction and is consistently faster than all existing solvers here. None of the frameworks have built-in sparsity support and, unsurprisingly, don’t scale well. However, with increasing discretization, explicitly forming Jacobians eventually becomes infeasible. Figure 11 demonstrates NonlinearSolve.jl Jacobian-free Krylov Methods with Preconditioning that solve the linear system without constructing the Jacobian. We note that Sundials KINSOL with their built-in GMRES fails to converge to the solution, and MINPACK and NLsolve.jl don’t support Jacobian-Free methods.

\ Finally, in Figure 12, we present a Work-Precision Diagram for the discretized Brusselator on a 32 × 32 grid, showing that our methods are an order of magnitude faster than the ones in other software.

items.title: Giant, Fungus-Like Organism May Be Completely Unknown Branch of Life
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
New research suggests that Prototaxites, once believed to be a giant fungus, may actually represent an entirely extinct and previously unknown branch of complex life, distinct from fungi, plants, animals, and protists. Live Science reports: The researchers studied the fossilized remains of one Prototaxites species named Prototaxites taiti, found preserved in the Rhynie chert, a sedimentary deposit of exceptionally well-preserved fossils of early land plants and animals in Scotland. This species was much smaller than many other species of Prototaxites, only growing up to a few inches tall, but it is still the largest Prototaxites specimen found in this region. Upon examining the internal structure of the fossilized Prototaxites, the researchers found that its interior was made up of a series of tubes, similar to those within a fungus. But these tubes branched off and reconnected in ways very unlike those seen in modern fungi. "We report that Prototaxites taiti was the largest organism in the Rhynie ecosystem and its anatomy was fundamentally distinct from all known extant or extinct fungi," the researchers wrote in the paper. "We therefore conclude that Prototaxites was not a fungus, and instead propose it is best assigned to a now entirely extinct terrestrial lineage."
 
True fungi from the same period have also been preserved in the Rhynie chert, enabling the researchers to chemically compare them to Prototaxites. In addition to their unique structural characteristics, the team found that the Prototaxites fossils left completely different chemical signatures to the fungi fossils, indicating that the Prototaxites did not contain chitin, a major building block of fungal cell walls and a hallmark of the fungal kingdom. The Prototaxites fossils instead appeared to contain chemicals similar to lignin, which is found in the wood and bark of plants. "We conclude that the morphology and molecular fingerprint of P. taiti is clearly distinct from that of the fungi and other organism preserved alongside it in the Rhynie chert, and we suggest that it is best considered a member of a previously undescribed, entirely extinct group of eukaryotes," the researchers wrote. The research has been published on the preprint server bioRxiv.

items.title: FDIC Rescinds Guidance Around Banks and Crypto
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) says banks no longer need prior approval before engaging in crypto-related activities, such as holding digital currency assets or partnering with companies in the industry. Axios reports: After publishing a general caution against banks participating in the industry just two years ago, the FDIC is the latest Trump administration regulator to change its tune entirely amid the president's warm embrace of crypto. "With today's action, the FDIC is turning the page on the flawed approach of the past three years," FDIC acting chairman Travis Hill said in a statement.
 
The OCC was the first of those regulators to revise their guidance, telling banks it supervises earlier this month that they no longer need permission to engage in certain common cryptocurrency-related activities. The Fed as of Friday had not issued any update, though chair Jerome Powell told lawmakers during a congressional hearing last month that the central bank would take a fresh look at the guidance. The new policy clarifies that "FDIC-supervised institutions may engage in permissible activities, including ... digital assets, provided that they adequately manage the associated risks."

items.title: A New Image File Format Efficiently Stores Invisible Light Data
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
An anonymous reader quotes a report from Ars Technica: Imagine working with special cameras that capture light your eyes can't even see -- ultraviolet rays that cause sunburn, infrared heat signatures that reveal hidden writing, or specific wavelengths that plants use for photosynthesis. Or perhaps using a special camera designed to distinguish the subtle visible differences that make paint colors appear just right under specific lighting. Scientists and engineers do this every day, and they're drowning in the resulting data. A new compression format called Spectral JPEG XL might finally solve this growing problem in scientific visualization and computer graphics. Researchers Alban Fichet and Christoph Peters of Intel Corporation detailed the format in a recent paper published in the Journal of Computer Graphics Techniques (JCGT). It tackles a serious bottleneck for industries working with these specialized images. These spectral files can contain 30, 100, or more data points per pixel, causing file sizes to balloon into multi-gigabyte territory -- making them unwieldy to store and analyze.
 
[...] The current standard format for storing this kind of data, OpenEXR, wasn't designed with these massive spectral requirements in mind. Even with built-in lossless compression methods like ZIP, the files remain unwieldy for practical work as these methods struggle with the large number of spectral channels. Spectral JPEG XL utilizes a technique used with human-visible images, a math trick called a discrete cosine transform (DCT), to make these massive files smaller. Instead of storing the exact light intensity at every single wavelength (which creates huge files), it transforms this information into a different form. [...]
 
According to the researchers, the massive file sizes of spectral images have reportedly been a real barrier to adoption in industries that would benefit from their accuracy. Smaller files mean faster transfer times, reduced storage costs, and the ability to work with these images more interactively without specialized hardware. The results reported by the researchers seem impressive -- with their technique, spectral image files shrink by 10 to 60 times compared to standard OpenEXR lossless compression, bringing them down to sizes comparable to regular high-quality photos. They also preserve key OpenEXR features like metadata and high dynamic range support. The report notes that broader adoption "hinges on the continued development and refinement of the software tools that handle JPEG XL encoding and decoding."
 
Some scientific applications may also see JPEG XL's lossy approach as a drawback. "Some researchers working with spectral data might readily accept the trade-off for the practical benefits of smaller files and faster processing," reports Ars. "Others handling particularly sensitive measurements might need to seek alternative methods of storage."

items.title: 6 Hidden Challenges of Powering Your Home With Renewable Energy
items.author: Zac Amos
items.content:
Rising utility costs and homeowners’ desire to decrease their carbon footprints have inspired an interest in residential renewable energy. However, while transitioning to clean power at home has many benefits for the planet and people’s wallets, it’s not always as simple as installing rooftop solar panels or a geothermal heating system. Homeowners may face these six challenges when powering their homes with renewables.

1. Initial Investments Are Costly

Before federal tax credits, state incentives, and manufacturer rebates kick in, the initial investment in renewable energy is costly. Without discount opportunities, homeowners pay about on an average-sized home. The federal tax credit and local incentives may decrease the cost to $20,552 or less, which is still outside many people’s budgets.

\ Other renewable energy sources are also pricey. On average, homeowners could for geothermal heat pumps, depending on the size of their house, required capacity, system type, and installation fees.

\ Some geothermal setups are even more expensive, like a horizontal closed-loop system, which costs between $15,000 and $34,000 but is ideal for rural properties. Meanwhile, a vertical closed-loop system could cost anywhere from $20,000 to $38,000 for rural or urban properties with limited space.

\ It’s still worth exploring federal tax credits to offset the costs of energy-efficient upgrades. Homeowners may be eligible for a through 2032.

2. Solar Power Is Weather-Dependent

Rooftop solar panels do an excellent job of providing clean energy to homes under ideal conditions. For example, they work best on a clear, sunny day with minimal cloud coverage and cooler temperatures. Even fog or shady trees can hinder how much sunlight the panels absorb, limiting power output.

\ Other weather conditions may also impact solar panels’ ability to provide power. Though wind can cool the system, it can also carry damaging debris. The same occurs with extreme weather events like hurricanes, tornadoes, heat waves, and freezing temperatures, which could adversely affect the rooftop solar system.

\ Though homeowners can’t control the weather, they can remove overhanging and dead trees to prevent branches from snapping and falling on the panels.

3. Limited Transmission Infrastructure Impacts Net Metering

Much of the United States’ grid transmission lines and transformers have , meaning they’ve hit capacity, particularly during peak demand. While engineers have begun replacing outdated lines, widespread modernization will require time and trillions of dollars in investments.

\ Limited transmission infrastructure may cause grid congestion — when there’s too little capacity to transfer excess residential renewable energy from homes to the grid. Homeowners on their electricity bills from their state’s net metering policies.

\ Additionally, net metering equates to a faster return on investment. Without it, homeowners’ payback period for their rooftop solar power system is much longer. They also remain reliant on traditional grid power even if their solar panels produce excess power, making their investment in clean energy less attractive.

\ To overcome this problem, homeowners should advocate for improved net metering policies with local utilities and consider alternatives like feed-in tariffs. Exploring community solar programs is another option if available.

4. Efficient Energy Storage Is Lacking

While renewable energy advancements are impressive, efficient at-home energy storage is still lacking. Sufficient power storage can retain excess renewable energy until homeowners need it. Its application is essential to and provide a backup power source during prolonged outages.

\ However, renewable energy storage has several limitations, including solar and wind intermittency, high costs, supply chain issues and limited life spans. With home batteries, the frequent degradation and replacement also pose a negative environmental risk.

\ Despite this, engineers are to overcome energy volume, safety, and storage duration — an improvement from lithium-ion batteries. Regulatory changes will also improve the development and distribution of these technologies.

\ While renewable energy storage is still in its infancy, homeowners should consider existing solutions. Integrating smart energy management software will further allow them to maximize solar storage capacity and efficiency and optimize the available energy.

5. Cyberattacks May Disrupt the Power Supply to Homes

Homeowners must contend with cybersecurity threats on the renewable energy supply as they would with any modern technology. The system requires an internet connection to control or monitor power outputs, thus increasing the risk of a cyberattack. Criminals often target inverters — the components used to — because of vulnerabilities posed by outdated software and insufficient passwords.

\ Cyberattackers can disrupt the power supply by tampering with renewable energy systems. The rising threat led the FBI to issue a warning in July 2024, recommending that businesses and individuals , update system access, look for abnormal activity, and regularly update antivirus software.

6. Renewable Power Systems Require Enough Space

According to SolarReviews, the average home — about 2,000 to 2,500 square feet — for sufficient power supply. This amount of panels equates to about 264 to 334 square feet of roof space.

\ Unfortunately, homeowners may face several hurdles when installing the correct number of panels. For example, a home with expansive square footage may have a narrow roof design or obstructions such as chimneys, vents, and skylights. Though high-efficiency solar panels can generate more power in less space, they are more expensive.

\ Geothermal heat pumps also have space requirements, depending on the type you install. For instance, horizontal arrays need approximately to fit the piping, which is far more than vertical arrays. In other words, a 1,500-square-foot home will need 3,750 square feet of land for horizontal heat pumps. Larger homes usually have enough outdoor space for these systems, but smaller homes or urban residences may not.

\ Homeowners may overcome space limitations by exploring creative vertical solutions, such as installing solar panels on walls and fences. Community solar projects with large off-site arrays may be another option. If possible, property owners should consult with a geothermal energy expert to determine if they can install vertical heat pumps instead.

Clean Residential Energy Is Still Worth the Investment

While several barriers to renewable energy adoption exist, there are always ways to overcome them. Many people find that switching to clean energy at home accrues massive energy savings and a better quality of life, regardless of the challenges.

items.author: Marina Temkin

items.title: DOGE To Rewrite SSA Codebase In 'Months'
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
Longtime Slashdot reader frank_adrian314159 writes: According to an article in Wired, Elon Musk has appointed a team of technologists from DOGE to "rewrite the code that runs the SSA in months." This codebase has over 60 million lines of COBOL and handles record keeping for all American workers and payments for all Social Security recipients. Given that the code has to track the byzantine regulations dealing with Social Security, it's no wonder that the codebase is this large. What is in question though is whether a small team can rewrite this code "in months." After all, what could possibly go wrong? "The project is being organized by Elon Musk lieutenant Steve Davis ... and aims to migrate all SSA systems off COBOL ... and onto a more modern replacement like Java within a scheduled tight timeframe of a few months," notes Wired.
 
"Under any circumstances, a migration of this size and scale would be a massive undertaking, experts tell WIRED, but the expedited deadline runs the risk of obstructing payments to the more than 65 million people in the US currently receiving Social Security benefits."
 
In 2017, SSA announced a plan to modernize its core systems with a timeline of around five years. However, the work was "pivoted away" because of the pandemic.

items.title: Oracle Health Breach Compromises Patient Data At US Hospitals
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
A breach of legacy Cerner servers at Oracle Health exposed patient data from multiple U.S. hospitals and healthcare organizations, with threat actors using compromised customer credentials to steal the data before it had been migrated to Oracle Cloud. Despite confirming the breach privately, Oracle Health has yet to publicly acknowledge the incident. BleepingComputer reports: Oracle Health, formerly known as Cerner, is a healthcare software-as-a-service (SaaS) company offering Electronic Health Records (EHR) and business operations systems to hospitals and healthcare organizations. After being acquired by Oracle in 2022, Cerner was merged into Oracle Health, with its systems migrated to Oracle Cloud. In a notice sent to impacted customers and seen by BleepingComputer, Oracle Health said it became aware of a breach of legacy Cerner data migration servers on February 20, 2025.
 
"We are writing to inform you that, on or around February 20, 2025, we became aware of a cybersecurity event involving unauthorized access to some amount of your Cerner data that was on an old legacy server not yet migrated to the Oracle Cloud," reads a notification sent to impacted Oracle Health customers. Oracle says that the threat actor used compromised customer credentials to breach the servers sometime after January 22, 2025, and copied data to a remote server. This stolen data "may" have included patient information from electronic health records. However, multiple sources told BleepingComputer that it was confirmed that patient data was stolen during the attack.
 
Oracle Health is also telling hospitals that they will not notify patients directly and that it is their responsibility to determine if the stolen data violates HIPAA laws and whether they are required to send notifications. However, the company says they will help identify impacted individuals and provide templates to help with notifications.

items.title: Beyond RGB: A new image file format efficiently stores invisible light data
items.author: Benj Edwards
items.content:
Imagine working with special cameras that capture light your eyes can't even see—ultraviolet rays that cause sunburn or infrared heat signatures that reveal hidden writing. Or perhaps using specialized cameras sensitive enough to distinguish subtle color variations in paint that look just right under specific lighting. Scientists and engineers do this every day—and they're drowning in the resulting data.

A new compression format called Spectral JPEG XL might finally solve this growing problem in scientific visualization and computer graphics. Researchers Alban Fichet and Christoph Peters of Intel Corporation detailed the format in a recent paper published in the Journal of Computer Graphics Techniques (JCGT). It tackles a serious bottleneck for industries working with these specialized images. These spectral files can contain 30, 100, or more data points per pixel, causing file sizes to balloon into multi-gigabyte territory—making them unwieldy to store and analyze.

When we think of digital images, we typically imagine files that store just three colors: red, green, and blue (RGB). This works well for everyday photos, but capturing the true color and behavior of light requires much more detail. Spectral images aim for this higher fidelity by recording light's intensity not just in broad RGB categories, but across dozens or even hundreds of narrow, specific wavelength bands. This detailed information primarily spans the visible spectrum and often extends into near-infrared and near-ultraviolet regions crucial for simulating how materials interact with light accurately.

items.title: xAI Acquires X
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
Elon Musk says its xAI company has acquired the social media platform X in an all-stock transaction. "The combination values xAI at $80 billion and X at $33 billion ($45 billion less $12 billion debt)," said Musk. He writes on X: Since its founding two years ago, xAI has rapidly become one of the leading AI labs in the world, building models and data centers at unprecedented speed and scale. X is the digital town square where more than 600M active users go to find the real-time source of ground truth and, in the last two years, has been transformed into one of the most efficient companies in the world, positioning it to deliver scalable future growth.
 
xAI and X's futures are intertwined. Today, we officially take the step to combine the data, models, compute, distribution and talent. This combination will unlock immense potential by blending xAI's advanced AI capability and expertise with X's massive reach. The combined company will deliver smarter, more meaningful experiences to billions of people while staying true to our core mission of seeking truth and advancing knowledge. This will allow us to build a platform that doesn't just reflect the world but actively accelerates human progress.
 
I would like to recognize the hardcore dedication of everyone at xAI and X that has brought us to this point. This is just the beginning. Thank you for your continued partnership and support.

items.title: Trump Pardons Founder of Electric Vehicle Start-Up Nikola, Trevor Milton
items.author: BeauHD
items.content:
An anonymous reader quotes a report from The Guardian: Trevor Milton, the founder of electric vehicle start-up Nikola who was sentenced to prison last year, was pardoned by Donald Trump late on Thursday, the White House confirmed on Friday. The pardon of Milton, who was sentenced to four years in prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology, could wipe out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for defrauded investors. Milton and his wife donated more than $1.8 million to a Trump re-election campaign fund less than a month before the November election, according to the Federal Election Commission.
 
At Milton's trial, prosecutors say a company video of a prototype truck appearing to be driven down a desert highway was actually a video of a non-functioning Nikola that had been rolled down a hill. Milton had not been incarcerated pending an appeal. Milton said late on Thursday on social media and via a press release that he had been pardoned by Trump. "I am incredibly grateful to President Trump for his courage in standing up for what is right and for granting me this sacred pardon of innocence," Milton said.
Here's a timeline of notable events surrounding Nikola:
 
June, 2016: Nikola Motor Receives Over 7,000 Preorders Worth Over $2.3 Billion For Its Electric Truck 
December, 2016: Nikola Motor Company Reveals Hydrogen Fuel Cell Truck With Range of 1,200 Miles 
February, 2020: Nikola Motors Unveils Hybrid Fuel-Cell Concept Truck With 600-Mile Range 
June, 2020: Nikola Founder Exaggerated the Capability of His Debut Truck 
September, 2020: Nikola Motors Accused of Massive Fraud, Ocean of Lies 
September, 2020: Nikola Admits Prototype Was Rolling Downhill In Promo Video 
September, 2020: Nikola Founder Trevor Milton Steps Down as Chairman in Battle With Short Seller 
October, 2020: Nikola Stock Falls 14 Percent After CEO Downplays Badger Truck Plans 
November, 2020: Nikola Stock Plunges As Company Cancels Badger Pickup Truck 
July, 2021: Nikola Founder Trevor Milton Indicted on Three Counts of Fraud 
December, 2021: EV Startup Nikola Agrees To $125 Million Settlement 
September, 2022: Nikola Founder Lied To Investors About Tech, Prosecutor Says in Fraud Trial

December, 2023: Nikola Founder Trevor Milton Sentenced To 4 Years For Securities Fraud 
February 19, 2025: Nikola Files for Bankruptcy With Plans To Sell Assets, Wind Down

items.content: <article>“Tesla Takedown” organizers have promised their biggest day of global action today, encouraging thousands to protest outside Tesla showrooms, dealerships, and even charging stations to peacefully object to Elon Musk’s role in slashing government spending.  As Tesla protests have spread, so has the backlash. Activists holding up signs are being conflated with masked vandals throwing […]</article>

items.title: How to Test for Neighborhood-Level Internet Disparities
items.author: The Markup
items.content:
At The Markup, we’re committed to doing everything we can to protect our readers from digital harm, write about the processes we develop, and share our work. We’re constantly working on improving digital security, respecting reader privacy, creating ethical and responsible user experiences, and making sure our site and tools are accessible.

\ This article was copublished with OpenNews, a community organization that helps journalists share knowledge and build relationships that transform their work. Sign up for its newsletter

\ In The Markup’s first citizen science project, we built tools and an experimental blueprint that enabled anyone to test for internet disparities in the U.S. without having to write a line of code. The same underlying tools are useful for any story that compares a dataset with demographic information from the American Community Survey (ACS). I’ll walk through the decisions we made for our investigation at The Markup in the hopes that this will help you test for neighborhood-level disparities for your next story.

In October 2022, The Markup released an investigation into internet disparities in major cities across the United States. Aaron Sankin and I found that four of the nation’s largest internet service providers (ISPs) charged the same monthly price for drastically different internet speeds in different parts of town. In all but two of the cities we tested, we found that neighborhoods with lower incomes, a lower rate of White residents, or which had been historically redlined (or a combination of those factors) were disproportionately offered “slow” speeds—which we defined as speeds below what the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) considers broadband internet—for the same price as faster speeds offered to neighborhoods that were richer or had more White residents.

\ At least nine local news outlets used our data to report on the digital divide in their cities, and the FCC asked for public comment on our findings as part of its congressionally-mandated effort to root out the causes of digital discrimination.

\ We worked on that investigation for eight months, and I spent a big chunk of it developing a method to collect representative samples of street addresses from 45 cities, merge socioeconomic data to the samples, and finally scrape internet service offers from four ISPs for almost one million individual addresses.

\ Shortly after we published, journalists, civil servants, and grassroots organizations asked us if we had collected data in the cities they lived in. Unfortunately, we limited our data collection to a 10% random sample of the largest city in each state served by at least one of the ISPs in our investigation. Moreover, our small team lacked the resources to maintain long-running data pipelines, let alone build new web scrapers for providers we hadn’t initially investigated.

\ Our investigation required time and technical muscle, which Aaron and I thought seemed like a big barrier to entry for others. So we invested a few months into building tools and straightforward steps to follow—so that anyone with internet and a computer could test for internet disparities in their neck of the woods. It’s a technique that can also help you report about other inequities in the communities you cover, from transit access to proximity to healthy food and health care.

How you can use this technique for your own project

There were two major technical hurdles we needed to address to make this project accessible to non-coders:

  1. Sampling street addresses anywhere in the U.S.
  2. Matching socioeconomic data to those addresses

\ With a search and a few clicks, you can build a sample of addresses from any city, county, state, or zip code, then export those addresses to a CSV file. (You can read more about why we built the tool, and try it out at usps.biglocalnews.org.)

\ Next, we needed to find a way to join addresseses with socioeconomic data. To do that, I built a custom Google Sheets template that allows users to import that CSV and automatically merge Census tract-level demographics using a simple formula. Although our initial investigation used block groups, we opted for Census tracts for this story recipe because they provide more reliable population estimates in less dense regions—and, more importantly, there are about 240,000 block groups in the U.S. but only about 85,000 tracts, which, with the formulas we wanted to run against them, was just barely manageable in Google Sheets.

Note: In our template, the I, J, and K columns are numerical codes representing the state, county, and Census tract of an address. We pad each code with the appropriate number of leading zeros to properly format the FIPS code of each Census tract.

\ We also tried merging the data based on Census tract codes using the classic function that referenced a separate tab in the spreadsheet with socioeconomic data organized by Census tracts. However, this proved to be too inefficient given the large number of tracts in the U.S. Luckily, my wife is an Excel wizard, and after laughing in my face over lunch, she showed me the performant magic of chaining and . By doing so, we were able to easily search the ACS tab (of more than 85,000 rows) to merge median household income and the percentage of non-Hispanic White residents for any Census tract:

We received our Census data as a well-formatted spreadsheet from Joe Germuska at the Northwestern University Knight Lab. He even calculated a reliability metric to warn users if the range of error was too high for a given Census tract.

Where to look for  story

We combined these steps in The Markup’s first “Build Your Own Dataset” guide, which is being used this summer by the Chicago community youth group Fresh Supply. Watch this video of Fresh Supply students collecting internet plans from AT&T:

\ This video requires JavaScript to play.

\ Beyond the original use cases, the tools we built have utility. You can use U.S. Place Sampler to collect random samples to test for address-level disparities for other topics. This could include utilities outages, access to grocery stores and medical centers, or the cost of rides across town using rideshare applications.

\ Note that these addresses from OpenAddresses mostly come from Enhanced 911 data used to direct dispatchers during emergencies. Some of the addresses may be outdated or incomplete. Even so, this is the most complete dataset of U.S. addresses to our knowledge.

\ You could even use the same spreadsheet template to merge socioeconomic data. You can collect other survey questions from the ACS and merge these new fields based on Census tract. The formula-chaining trick mentioned earlier will come in handy if your project requires manual data entry.

\ In our initial investigation, we also looked at historical redlining grades using digitized maps from the phenomenal Mapping Inequality project. If you’re a coder, we have a code snippet on our GitHub that you can use to determine if an address’s geographic coordinates fall within a graded neighborhood.

\ If you teach students in data journalism, statistics, or computational social science, the Build Your Own Dataset guide, or any of the tools we mentioned, would make great class projects. In fact, all of this started with an idea to create a guide that any high school science class could use.

But those startups won’t necessarily be financial technology companies. In a post on X, Rotman — who helped start QED in 2007 — declared that […]</article>

items.title: Nearly Half of People in the US Have Toxic PFAS in Their Drinking Water
items.author: msmash
items.content:
An anonymous reader shares a report: New data recently released by the Environmental Protection Agency indicate that more than 158 million people across the U.S. have drinking water contaminated by toxic "forever chemicals," scientifically known as perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS). 

"Drinking water is a major source of PFAS exposure. The sheer number of contaminated sites shows that these chemicals are likely present in most of the U.S. water supply," said David Andrews, deputy director of investigations and a senior scientist at the Environmental Working Group (EWG), a nonprofit advocacy organization, in a recent press release.

items.title: Smart TVs Are Employing Screen Monitoring Tech To Harvest User Data
items.author: msmash
items.content:
Smart TV platforms are increasingly monitoring what appears on users' screens through Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) technology, building detailed viewer profiles for targeted advertising. 

Roku, which transitioned from a hardware company to an advertising powerhouse, reported $3.5 billion in annual ad revenue for 2024 -- representing 85% of its total income. The company has aggressively acquired ACR-related firms, with Roku-owned technology winning an Emmy in 2023 for advancements in the field. 

According to market research firm Antenna, 43% of all streaming subscriptions in the United States were ad-supported by late 2024, showing the industry's shift toward advertising-based models. Most users unknowingly consent to this monitoring when setting up their devices. Though consumers can technically disable ACR in their TV settings, doing so often restricts functionality.

items.title: Enterprise HCM Implementation: A Transformation Journey Of Mohammed Misbahul Khair
items.author: Kashvi Pandey
items.content:
\ In an era where digital transformation initiatives often struggle with complexity and user adoption challenges, the remarkable success of the Oracle Fusion HCM implementation at Harbison Walker International (HWI) stands as a testament to exceptional technical leadership and operational excellence. Under the guidance of Mohammed Misbahul Khair, a leading Oracle HCM Solutions Architect, this ambitious enterprise-wide implementation has set new benchmarks for efficiency, user adoption, and operational cost savings in the HR technology sector, fundamentally transforming how the organization manages its global workforce.

\ The comprehensive implementation, encompassing Core HR, Payroll, Time and Labor, Absence Management, Benefits, and Recruiting modules, emerged as a significant challenge in enterprise HR transformation. With responsibility for managing the implementation across multiple geographies and supporting over 5,000 employees, Mohammed Misbahul Khair faced the complex task of coordinating various functional streams while maintaining strict quality controls and achieving aggressive efficiency targets. The project's scope required careful consideration of diverse regional requirements, compliance standards, and the intricate interplay between different HCM modules.

\ At the heart of this success story was a methodical approach to solution design and implementation. As a key implementation consultant, Mohammed implemented rigorous technical controls and innovative operational strategies that not only met but substantially exceeded performance targets. Through extensive business process workshops and detailed requirement gathering sessions, he established a robust foundation for the implementation. His thorough understanding of both technical capabilities and business needs enabled him to design solutions that precisely aligned with organizational objectives while leveraging Oracle Fusion HCM's full potential.

\ The implementation featured sophisticated configurations of employee and manager dashboards, carefully structured workforce hierarchies, and meticulously designed security frameworks. Mohammed's expertise in utilizing Oracle Functional Setup Manager enabled the deployment of complex workforce structures, including Legal Entity HCM Information and Enterprise HCM Info, creating a seamless and secure user experience. The project achieved an unprecedented 98% data accuracy rate during migration – a significant accomplishment in enterprise-scale data transitions. This success was attributed to Mohammed's careful planning and execution of data conversions using HCM Data Loader (HDL), ensuring the accurate migration of critical data including workforce structures, employee records, jobs, and locations.

\ The implementation's success translated into substantial operational improvements, with a 30% reduction in manual effort across HR processes, 25% improvement in payroll processing time, 40% increase in system utilization rates, and 20% reduction in overall HR operational costs. The project introduced several groundbreaking technical solutions, including sophisticated HCM Extracts and Fast Formulas that revolutionized payroll processes, enabling efficient handling of retro payments, complex proration methods, and automated payment generation.

\ Stakeholder management emerged as a critical success factor throughout the implementation. Regular business process workshops and CRP sessions fostered strong stakeholder engagement, ensuring perfect alignment between technical solutions and business requirements. Mohammed's expertise in Oracle Fusion HCM allowed him to provide valuable insights and best-practice guidance, establishing him as a trusted advisor throughout the implementation lifecycle. The implementation prioritized user experience, resulting in intuitive interfaces and streamlined workflows that significantly enhanced adoption rates.

\ The achievement has demonstrated far-reaching implications for the HR technology sector, establishing new benchmarks for enterprise HCM projects, particularly in areas of data migration accuracy, user adoption rates, process automation efficiency, security framework design, and global workforce management. The implemented solution was designed with scalability and future innovation in mind, incorporating flexible configurations that could adapt to evolving business needs and technological advances.

\ For Mohammed Misbahul Khair personally, the project represented a significant career milestone, showcasing his ability to deliver complex technical solutions while managing substantial organizational change. His approach combined deep technical knowledge with strong business acumen, ensuring solutions that not only met current needs but also supported future growth and innovation in HR technology.

\ This success story illustrates how strategic technical leadership, when combined with effective implementation practices, can transform enterprise HR operations. The HWI implementation not only contributed to the organization's digital transformation but also established new standards for HCM implementation in the sector. As organizations continue to modernize their HR systems, this project serves as a compelling example of how focused leadership can drive exceptional results in enterprise-scale implementations.

About Mohammed Misbahul Khair

A distinguished professional in Oracle HCM solutions, Mohammed Misbahul Khair has established himself as a leading expert in enterprise HR technology implementation. Based in Edison, New Jersey, he holds a Master's degree in Information Technology Management and extensive Oracle certifications. His comprehensive experience spans implementation of Core HR, Talent Management, Recruiting, and Payroll modules, consistently delivering solutions that enhance organizational efficiency while improving the employee experience. His technical expertise extends across the full spectrum of Oracle Fusion HCM modules, with particular strength in Core HR, Global HR implementations, payroll processing and automation, benefits administration, talent management solutions, recruiting and onboarding systems, time and labor management, absence management, and security and compliance frameworks.

\ Through his career, Mohammed has demonstrated an exceptional ability to bridge the gap between technical capabilities and business requirements, consistently delivering solutions that drive organizational success while maintaining the highest standards of technical excellence. As the HR technology sector continues to evolve, his work at HWI stands as a model for future enterprise HCM projects, showcasing the powerful combination of technical expertise, operational excellence, and business acumen in driving transformational change in enterprise HR operations.

:::tip This story was distributed as a release by Kashvi Pandey under HackerNoon’s Business Blogging Program. Learn more about the program .

items.title: Scientists Propose 'Bodyoids' To Address Medical Research and Organ Shortage Challenges
items.author: msmash
items.content:
Stanford University researchers have proposed creating "bodyoids" -- ethically sourced human bodies grown from stem cells without neural components for consciousness or pain sensation -- to revolutionize medical research and address organ shortages. In a new opinion piece published in MIT Technology Review, scientists Carsten T. Charlesworth, Henry T. Greely, and Hiromitsu Nakauchi argue that recent advances in biotechnology make this concept increasingly plausible. The approach would combine pluripotent stem cells, artificial uterus technology, and genetic techniques to inhibit brain development. 

The researchers point to persistent shortages of human biological materials as a major bottleneck in medical progress. More than 100,000 patients currently await solid organ transplants in the US alone, while less than 15% of drugs entering clinical trials receive regulatory approval. These lab-grown bodies could potentially generate patient-specific organs that are perfect immunological matches, eliminate the need for lifelong immunosuppression, and provide personalized drug screening models.

items.title: Oracle has reportedly suffered 2 separate breaches exposing thousands of customers‘ PII
items.author: Dan Goodin
items.content:
Oracle isn’t commenting on recent reports that it has experienced two separate data breaches that have exposed sensitive personal information belonging to thousands of its customers.

The most recent data breach report, published Friday by Bleeping Computer, said that Oracle Health—a health care software-as-a-service business the company acquired in 2022—had learned in February that a threat actor accessed one of its servers and made off with patient data from US hospitals. Bleeping Computer said Oracle Health customers have received breach notifications that were printed on plain paper rather than official Oracle letterhead and were signed by Seema Verma, the executive vice president & GM of Oracle Health.

The other report of a data breach occurred eight days ago, when an anonymous person using the handle rose87168 published a sampling of what they said were 6 million records of authentication data belonging to Oracle Cloud customers. Rose87168 told Bleeping Computer that they had acquired the data a little more than a month earlier after exploiting a vulnerability that gave access to an Oracle Cloud server.

items.title: Revolutionizing Enterprise Security: Sasibhushana Matcha's FIDO Implementation
items.author: Kashvi Pandey
items.content:
\ In an era where cybersecurity threats pose increasingly complex challenges to enterprise systems, Sasibhushana Matcha's innovative implementation of FIDO (Fast Identity Online) authentication stands as a breakthrough in secure, passwordless access solutions. As a distinguished Principal Application Engineer and enterprise architect, Sasibhushana has successfully transformed the traditional approach to authentication security through cutting-edge microservices architecture and cloud-native implementation, setting new standards for enterprise security solutions.

\ The groundbreaking project, which reimagines authentication through biometric integration including FaceID, TouchID, and advanced passkey systems, showcases Sasibhushana's exceptional ability to bridge sophisticated technical solutions with practical enterprise needs. Leading the technical architecture and implementation, Sasibhushana designed a comprehensive suite of microservices that seamlessly integrate FIDO protocols into enterprise systems, establishing new standards for secure authentication in cloud environments.

\ At the core of this innovation is a robust microservices architecture that handles both FIDO registration and authentication processes. Sasibhushana's strategic approach to system design emphasizes scalability and security, implementing advanced CICD pipelines in OpenShift that automate critical testing phases - from unit tests to performance evaluation and post-deployment smoke tests. This comprehensive testing strategy ensures unwavering system reliability while maintaining the highest security standards.

\ The architecture's sophistication lies in its modular design, allowing for seamless integration of various biometric authentication methods while maintaining strict security protocols. By leveraging containerization and orchestration through OpenShift, Sasibhushana's implementation ensures high availability and resilience, critical factors for enterprise-grade authentication systems.

\ The project's technical sophistication is matched by its practical impact on enterprise security. By eliminating the vulnerabilities associated with traditional password systems, Sasibhushana's implementation significantly reduces the risk of data breaches while enhancing user experience through seamless biometric authentication. This balance of security and usability demonstrates Sasibhushana's deep understanding of enterprise needs and his ability to deliver solutions that drive tangible business value.

\ The FIDO implementation introduces multiple layers of security while maintaining a frictionless user experience. Through careful architecture design, the system handles cryptographic operations securely on user devices, eliminating the risks associated with server-side password storage. This approach not only enhances security but also simplifies compliance with data protection regulations.

\ Sasibhushana's expertise in cloud architecture played a crucial role in the project's success. The implementation leverages cloud-native technologies to ensure scalability and reliability, with carefully designed microservices that handle authentication requests efficiently across distributed systems. The integration of automated testing pipelines ensures continuous quality assurance, with each deployment passing through rigorous security and performance evaluations.

\ The system's cloud-native architecture enables seamless scaling to handle varying authentication loads while maintaining consistent performance. Through careful optimization of container resources and network communication, the implementation achieves remarkable response times for authentication requests, enhancing the user experience without compromising security.

\ The success of this FIDO implementation represents a significant milestone in enterprise security architecture, showcasing how strategic technical leadership can transform fundamental aspects of digital security. As organizations continue to grapple with evolving security threats, Sasibhushana's work serves as a blueprint for modern authentication systems that prioritize both security and user experience.

\ The implementation has demonstrated significant improvements in security metrics, with early adopters reporting substantial reductions in authentication-related security incidents. The system's ability to eliminate password-related vulnerabilities while maintaining high usability has garnered attention from security professionals across the industry.

\ The implications of this project extend beyond immediate security enhancements. By successfully implementing passwordless authentication at an enterprise scale, Sasibhushana has demonstrated how innovative architectural approaches can solve complex security challenges while improving operational efficiency. His work continues to influence the evolution of enterprise security solutions, setting new standards for authentication systems in cloud-native environments.

\ The architecture's forward-thinking design accommodates future authentication methods and security protocols, ensuring long-term viability and adaptability. This flexibility allows organizations to evolve their security measures without significant architectural changes, providing a sustainable solution for enterprise authentication needs.

\ As a trusted authority in enterprise architecture and digital innovation, Sasibhushana Matcha's contributions to this project reflect his broader impact on the field of digital transformation. His holistic approach to system architecture, combined with deep technical expertise, continues to shape the future of enterprise software development, focusing on creating scalable, secure, and efficient solutions that drive business value in an increasingly complex digital landscape.

\ The project exemplifies Sasibhushana's commitment to excellence in enterprise architecture, demonstrating how thoughtful system design can address complex security challenges while maintaining operational efficiency. His innovative approach to implementing FIDO authentication serves as a model for organizations seeking to modernize their security infrastructure while enhancing user experience.

About Sasibhushana Matcha

\ Sasibhushana Matcha is a distinguished Principal Application Engineer and enterprise architect whose career has been marked by significant contributions to cloud computing and distributed systems architecture. Holding an AWS Solutions Architect certification and a Master's in Computer Applications, he brings a comprehensive understanding of both theoretical principles and practical implementation challenges in enterprise software development.

\ His expertise spans a wide range of technologies, including Java/J2EE, cloud platforms, microservices architecture, and enterprise systems integration. Throughout his career, Sasibhushana has demonstrated exceptional ability in bridging complex technical concepts with business objectives, making him an invaluable asset to organizations undergoing digital transformation.

\ As a Principal Application Engineer, Sasibhushana has consistently shown an unwavering commitment to innovation and excellence. His work in implementing cloud-native solutions and modernizing legacy systems has helped numerous organizations successfully navigate their digital transformation journeys. His holistic approach to system architecture, combined with deep understanding of enterprise needs, continues to influence the evolution of cloud-native solutions and distributed systems architecture.

\ Sasibhushana's contributions to the field extend beyond technical implementation, as he actively participates in knowledge sharing and mentoring within the technical community. His thought leadership in enterprise architecture and security solutions continues to shape industry practices, establishing him as a respected voice in the evolution of enterprise software development.

items.title: Again and Again, NSO Group's Customers Keep Getting Their Spyware Operations Caught
items.author: msmash
items.content:
An anonymous reader shares a report: Amnesty International published a new report this week detailing attempted hacks against two Serbian journalists, allegedly carried out with NSO Group's spyware Pegasus. The two journalists, who work for the Serbia-based Balkan Investigative Reporting Network (BIRN), received suspicious text messages including a link -- basically a phishing attack, according to the nonprofit. In one case, Amnesty said its researchers were able to click on the link in a safe environment and see that it led to a domain that they had previously identified as belonging to NSO Group's infrastructure. 

"Amnesty International has spent years tracking NSO Group Pegasus spyware and how it has been used to target activists and journalists," Donncha O Cearbhaill, the head of Amnesty's Security Lab, told TechCrunch. "This technical research has allowed Amnesty to identify malicious websites used to deliver the Pegasus spyware, including the specific Pegasus domain used in this campaign." 

To his point, security researchers like O Cearbhaill who have been keeping tabs on NSO's activities for years are now so good at spotting signs of the company's spyware that sometimes all researchers have to do is quickly look at a domain involved in an attack. In other words, NSO Group and its customers are losing their battle to stay in the shadows. "NSO has a basic problem: They are not as good at hiding as their customers think," John Scott-Railton, a senior researcher at The Citizen Lab, a human rights organization that has investigated spyware abuses since 2012, told TechCrunch.

items.title: Nintendo used its new app to announce the ‘Legend of Zelda’ movie release date
items.author: Amanda Silberling
items.content: <article>Nintendo announced on Friday that its live-action “The Legend of Zelda” movie will premiere on March 26, 2027. It’s a little weird to announce this the day after a Nintendo Direct livestream, where the gaming company usually unloads all of its recent news. But that was intentional. The company wants fans to download its new […]</article>

items.title: UK Govt Data People Not Technical, Says Ex-Downing St Data Science Head
items.author: msmash
items.content:
An anonymous reader shares a report: A former director of data science at the UK prime minister's office has told MPs that people working with data in government are not typically technical and would be unlikely to get a similar job in the private sector. 

In a hearing designed to illuminate the challenges facing the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) as it strives to become the digital centre for government, MPs quizzed Laura Gilbert, head of AI for Government, at the Ellison Institute and former director of data science at 10 Downing Street, the prime ministers' office. 

Members of the House of Common's Science, Innovation and Technology Committee wanted to know about the performance of the Government Digital Service, which in January was moved from the Cabinet Office to DSIT and merged with Central Digital and Data Office (CDDO), the Incubator for AI (i.AI). Gilbert, a particle physicist who has worked in a number of tech industry roles, said one of the challenges was understanding the level of tech skills in the civil service in central government.

items.title: Siddhey Mahadik's Leadership in Call of Duty Security Development
items.author: Kashvi Pandey
items.content:
\ In an era where online gaming security faces unprecedented challenges from sophisticated cheating methods and evolving cyber threats, the remarkable achievements of Siddhey Mahadik in leading Activision's Gaming Security Team for Call of Duty: Modern Warfare III stand as a testament to innovation and technical excellence. Through his role as Scrum Master and security development leader, Mahadik has set new benchmarks for game security implementation, particularly in combating cheating and ensuring fair play for millions of players worldwide. His work has become increasingly crucial as the gaming industry grapples with the rise of artificial intelligence-powered cheating tools and sophisticated hacking attempts.

\ The ambitious security enhancement project for Call of Duty 2023 and 2024 editions represented a significant challenge in modern game development, particularly given the franchise's massive player base and the complexity of maintaining security across multiple platforms. With responsibility for managing a team of 15 cross-functional engineers and coordinating across multiple game development studios, Mahadik faced the intricate task of implementing cutting-edge security measures while maintaining the game's performance and user experience. This delicate balance required innovative approaches to security implementation that could scale effectively across millions of concurrent players without impacting gameplay performance.

\ At the heart of this success story was a sophisticated approach to machine learning implementation and agile project management. As the Scrum Master for the Machine Learning Team, Mahadik spearheaded the development of advanced anomaly detection systems that achieved an impressive 90% success rate in identifying and flagging cheating patterns in real-time – a significant advancement in gaming security technology. This system utilized state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms to analyze player behavior patterns, movement data, and game statistics to identify potential cheating activities with unprecedented accuracy.

\ The technical implementation involved creating robust data pipelines capable of processing massive amounts of gameplay data in real-time, developing sophisticated machine learning models that could adapt to new cheating methods, and implementing efficient deployment systems that could push security updates without disrupting the gaming experience. Mahadik's team pioneered new approaches to feature engineering that significantly improved the accuracy of cheat detection while minimizing false positives – a critical balance in maintaining player satisfaction.

\ The impact of his leadership extended far beyond technical metrics. Through strategic planning and efficient resource allocation, the project consistently remained 20% ahead of schedule – a remarkable achievement in complex software development. This accelerated timeline was achieved while maintaining high standards of code quality and security implementation, demonstrating exceptional project management capabilities. The team's ability to maintain this pace while dealing with the inherent complexities of game security development showcased Mahadik's skill in balancing competing priorities and managing technical debt effectively.

\ Cross-studio collaboration played a crucial role in the project's success. By partnering with three game development studios, Mahadik facilitated the enhancement of machine learning pipelines across multiple Call of Duty titles, including Modern Warfare III and Black Ops 6. This collaborative approach resulted in improved detection accuracy and more robust security measures across the franchise. The partnership model he established became a template for future cross-studio collaborations within Activision, demonstrating the value of shared knowledge and resources in tackling complex technical challenges.

\ The achievement garnered recognition across organizational levels, with the successful implementation of biweekly sprints and continuous product iteration maintaining a steady 2% improvement rate while meeting all security benchmarks. The project's success has become a model for future game security implementations, demonstrating how effective leadership and strategic project management can deliver exceptional results in gaming security. The team's ability to maintain consistent progress while adapting to new security challenges showed the robustness of the development framework Mahadik established.

\ In the realm of technical innovation, Mahadik's team pioneered several breakthrough approaches to game security. They developed novel methods for detecting and preventing memory manipulation in real-time, implemented advanced encryption protocols for sensitive game data, and created new techniques for identifying and blocking unauthorized third-party tools. These innovations not only improved security but also contributed to the broader field of game security research and development.

\ The project's success in implementing machine learning solutions for security represented a significant advancement in the gaming industry's approach to anti-cheat measures. By leveraging sophisticated algorithms and vast amounts of gameplay data, Mahadik's team created systems capable of learning and adapting to new cheating methods as they emerged. This proactive approach to security represented a paradigm shift from traditional reactive security measures, setting new standards for the industry.

\ For Siddhey Mahadik personally, the project represented a significant milestone in game security development, showcasing his ability to lead complex technical initiatives while managing substantial cross-functional teams. His success in developing the 2024 product roadmap for game security initiatives, incorporating comprehensive risk assessments and mitigation strategies, demonstrated his capability in strategic planning and execution. The roadmap he developed became a blueprint for future security initiatives across multiple game titles.

\ This success story illustrates how strategic leadership, when combined with cutting-edge technology and effective project management practices, can transform game security implementation. The Modern Warfare III security enhancement project not only contributed to creating a fairer gaming environment but also established new standards for security implementation in the gaming industry. The project's success in balancing robust security measures with optimal game performance demonstrated the power of innovative technical solutions in addressing complex gaming challenges.

\ Looking ahead, the implications of this project success extend beyond immediate achievements. It demonstrates how effective project management and innovative technical solutions can overcome complex security challenges while delivering exceptional value to players and stakeholders. As the gaming industry continues to evolve, this project stands as a model for future game security implementations, showcasing the powerful combination of machine learning technology, strategic leadership, and operational excellence in driving project success under Siddhey Mahadik's capable leadership.

\ A distinguished professional in game security development, Siddhey Mahadik has established himself as a leading expert in implementing machine learning-driven security solutions for major gaming titles. His comprehensive experience spans across security development and project management for high-profile games, including successful delivery of critical security updates for multiple Call of Duty releases. With expertise in machine learning implementation and agile methodologies, Mahadik has demonstrated exceptional ability in optimizing development workflows, implementing innovative security measures, and ensuring superior quality control standards. His proficiency in integrating advanced machine learning models and facilitating cross-studio collaboration has consistently delivered operational excellence while maintaining the integrity of gaming experiences for millions of players worldwide.

\ His technical expertise extends beyond traditional game security measures to encompass cutting-edge applications of artificial intelligence and machine learning in gaming security. Mahadik's innovative approaches to security implementation have not only enhanced the gaming experience for millions of players but have also contributed to the broader evolution of security practices in the gaming industry. His work continues to influence how gaming companies approach security challenges in an increasingly complex digital landscape.

items.title: Inside YouTube's Weird World Of Fake Movie Trailers
items.author: msmash
items.content:
Fake movie trailers created with AI are proliferating across YouTube, with some garnering more views than official studio releases -- and Hollywood studios are quietly profiting from the phenomenon rather than shutting it down. Instead of enforcing copyright on these unauthorized videos, Warner Bros. Discovery, Sony Pictures, and Paramount are claiming monetization rights, directing ad revenue from fake trailers for films like "Superman" and "Gladiator II" into studio coffers, according to a Deadline investigation published Friday. 

YouTube channels like Screen Culture, which has amassed 1.4 billion views, merge official footage with AI-generated imagery to create convincing trailer mockups that frequently rank higher in search results than legitimate studio releases. "Monetizing unauthorized, unwanted, and subpar uses of human-centered IP is a race to the bottom," SAG-AFTRA told Deadline, condemning studios for profiting from content that exploits performers without permission.

items.title: Want To Go To College? Pay the College Board
items.author: msmash
items.content:
The College Board, described as a $2 billion nonprofit, functions as the primary gatekeeper for academic success within American higher education, according to an analysis by Bloomberg. The organization significantly shapes university admissions by controlling not only who gains entry to college but also influencing what students know upon arrival. 

This central role in managing and defining higher education admissions positions the Board uniquely. The story adds: The College Board writes the curriculum for 40 AP courses, administers and grades the exams, oversees the PSAT and SAT, and offers a variety of free and paid resources to help prepare for the courses and tests. Many students will wind up paying the company north of $1,000 over the course of their high school career. "If the same people can create the content and create the tests, that's a really great business model where you've got the whole public secondary education system wrapped up in one little company," says Jon Boeckenstedt, the vice provost of enrollment management at Oregon State University and a prominent critic of the College Board. 

Housing so many parts of the high school experience under one roof has made the New York-based organization immensely wealthy, with more than $1 billion in annual revenue -- on which it pays no taxes as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. But mere money isn't the biggest source of the College Board's might. Twelve decades after its creation, it's now the closest thing the fragmented American educational system has to a central governing body, with a huge amount of authority over what students are expected to know when they get to college. Higher education is arguably the most important driver of social mobility, as well as the most powerful force in selecting which members of the next generation will set the political and cultural agenda. By controlling who gets in and what they know when they get there, the College Board has become the chief gatekeeper of academic success in America.
