items.title: Web Scraping in 2025: Staying on Track with New Rules
items.author: DataImpulse
items.content:
\ Web scraping streamlines data collection from websites, aiding in competitor analysis, content migration, and machine learning training. While scraping public data is legal, businesses continue to tighten restrictions.

\ So, is it really a ? In this article, we’ll break it down and share practical tips to keep you ahead.

AI is no secret - its rapid development has led to smarter, more adaptive anti-scraping systems. As AI advances, anti-bot systems are becoming more aggressive. The result? Scrapers often come across unexpected roadblocks.

\ Bot detection powered by AI analyzes patterns and behaviors that set bots apart from human users, including factors like IP address activity, browsing speed, mouse movements, and other signals. AI algorithms can adapt to the techniques bots use. As a result, even when scrapers change tactics - such as simulating human actions - the AI can still spot unusual patterns, triggering CAPTCHAs or blocking the request altogether. Moreover, AI can analyze a wider range of data sources to identify anomalies, like frequent requests from the same IP in a short time, which is a common sign of scraping.

\ Another aspect is advanced CAPTCHAs. They combine multiple layers of defense, from object recognition and behavioral analysis to machine learning. Some systems mix CAPTCHAs with two-factor authentication (2FA). It means that even if a bot bypasses a CAPTCHA challenge, additional layers of security will block it. Some websites may integrate biometric verification or use cryptographic puzzles.

\ Next, it’s a technique that intentionally makes JavaScript code difficult to read. JavaScript-based obfuscation is a method where the code written in JavaScript is altered to make it more complex, which makes it difficult for scrapers to extract data. In 2025 and beyond, obfuscation may become part of a more comprehensive anti-scraping strategy, combining with CAPTCHAs, AI-based bot detection, and behavioral analysis to create a multi-layered defense against automated scraping.

\ If you're a developer working with a company providing access to financial data, you'll need an API key, which will authenticate the request and ensure valid access. OAuth and API keys are two common authentication methods. When a user logs into an app via their Google or Facebook account, OAuth is used to grant permission to the app to access their profile information or social media data without sharing their password. These methods will remain key for businesses to secure data and maintain user privacy, all while supporting third-party developer partnerships.

\ In 2025, platforms will use advanced fingerprinting and IP blocking to prevent scraping. Services like Netflix, Hulu, and BBC iPlayer will continue to employ geo-blocking, making it harder for scrapers to access restricted content. To bypass these, rotating proxies and VPNs will be a must-have but more challenging to manage.

What’s on the Legal Side?

One of the most significant factors affecting the future of web scraping is the increase in data privacy laws worldwide. Governments are tightening regulations on the collection, processing, and storage of personal data.

\ The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) in the European Union, for example, requires organizations to obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data. This has significantly impacted web scraping, particularly for websites dealing with personal information.

\ Furthermore, Terms of Service (ToS) agreements are increasingly rigorous, as numerous platforms clearly ban scraping activities. Companies like Amazon, Google, and eBay have taken legal action against violators, enforcing strict rules around scraping product listings, reviews, and exclusive data. Consequently, many companies are prioritizing the compliance of third-party data sources with both local and international laws.

\ You might think that scraping without legal permission could offer short-term benefits, but it's important to think twice. Always review the Terms of Service and ensure your actions are in compliance. It's better to be safe than sorry.

Put an End to Scraping Headaches

As you can see, the rules are constantly evolving, but for any professional, this shouldn’t be a problem. Here are some smart strategies to manage the growing difficulties in web scraping.

  1. First things first - rotating proxies. They are unique IPs of real devices. Websites rarely detect such proxies, so users can scrape data without blocks. Traffic appears natural when routed through residential proxies. At DataImpulse, you can also test residential proxies with full customization options. 
  2. Use AI-powered CAPTCHA solvers. These tools incorporate advanced algorithms to crack complex puzzles. Overall, AI solvers can handle CAPTCHAs by using improved recognition and machine learning capabilities.
  3. Encrypt your traffic. Consider using TLS for secure connections and use HTTP/2 headers to speed up your requests.
  4. If possible, use official APIs to reduce legal risks and minimize detection instead of traditional scraping.
  5. Set randomized request intervals. Mimic human browsing behavior by setting random intervals between requests. 
  6. Choose distributed scraping for large-scale tasks. Run scrapers on multiple cloud servers or edge computing nodes to balance traffic and reduce suspicion.
  7. Use ISP and mobile proxies. They provide better anonymity and are less likely to get blocked (compared to datacenter proxies). 
  8. Implement fingerprint spoofing. Modify browser fingerprints (user agents, canvas, WebGL, etc.) 
  9. Work with legal experts. You can have a consultation with legal professionals to understand the possible outcomes of web scraping. 

\ One of the most important bits of advice is to make sure you're following GDPR(General Data Protection Regulation) or CCPA regulations to avoid legal trouble!

Proxies to Future-Proof Your Scraping

So, what proxies should you choose for web scraping tasks? The best options for reliable, efficient scraping are residential and mobile proxies. By using IPs from genuine devices, residential proxies blend with everyday web traffic, reducing the chances of being flagged by anti-scraping systems. Mobile proxies use IPs from real mobile devices, which are even harder to track and are often ignored by anti-scraping systems. These proxies let you spread traffic across different IPs and locations.

\ Simply using proxies is not enough. We recommend keeping a close eye on the health of your proxies. Regularly monitor their performance for issues like slow response times, blacklisting, or high failure rates. This proactive approach can help avoid disruptions that could hinder your scraping operations.

Web scraping is a valuable tool, but it still comes with challenges due to AI advancements and other factors. Keep on track with all the latest developments, adapt to new rules, and leverage the right tools and strategies. Hope this article will help you handle web scraping hiccups in 2025. Stay tuned with DataImpulse 🚀

items.title: Revolutionizing Data Center Deployment Through Automation: Aditya Mehra's VCF Orchestration Success
items.author: Kashvi Pandey
items.content:
\ In an era where digital transformation initiatives demand ever-increasing agility and efficiency, the groundbreaking work led by Aditya Mehra in VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) Day 1 orchestration stands as a compelling example of innovation in enterprise cloud infrastructure. This ambitious automation project has set new standards for deployment efficiency, cost reduction, and operational excellence in software-defined data center (SDDC) implementations across the technology landscape.

\ The challenge was significant and multifaceted: traditional SDDC deployments were mired in manual processes that typically consumed up to four days of intensive work, creating an environment prone to human error, technical inconsistencies, and substantial operational inefficiencies. These lengthy deployment cycles represented not just a technical hurdle but a genuine business constraint that limited organizational responsiveness to changing market conditions. Recognizing this critical bottleneck in enterprise cloud adoption, Aditya Mehra spearheaded a comprehensive initiative to revolutionize the deployment workflow through sophisticated automation and orchestration techniques that would fundamentally transform infrastructure delivery capabilities.

\ At the core of this transformation was Aditya's strategic approach to system integration and workflow design. As the technical lead, he architected an elegant solution that leveraged SDDC Manager's capabilities to automate the entire deployment chain—from initial provisioning and networking configuration to certificate management and workload domain creation. This holistic approach eliminated numerous manual touchpoints that had previously served as potential failure points in the deployment process. The attention to detail in Aditya's implementation ensured that each component of the complex deployment sequence was optimized for both reliability and performance, creating a seamless end-to-end experience.

\ The impact of Aditya Mehra's leadership extended far beyond technical implementation into the realm of operational sustainability. By successfully integrating VMware Customer Connect into the automation framework, he established a foundation for continuous improvement through seamless patch management and software lifecycle automation. This forward-thinking approach ensured that deployed environments would maintain optimal performance and security posture throughout their operational lifecycle. Additionally, Aditya's implementation of robust backup and disaster recovery mechanisms significantly enhanced system resilience, providing enterprise-grade protection against potential disruptions or data loss scenarios.

\ The monitoring and alerting infrastructure implemented by Aditya represented another dimension of operational excellence. These systems provided real-time visibility into deployment progress and system health, enabling proactive management of the infrastructure landscape and early identification of potential issues before they could impact business operations. This commitment to visibility and transparency established a new paradigm for infrastructure management within the organization.

\ Perhaps most impressive were the tangible results achieved through this initiative. The automated orchestration reduced deployment time by approximately 80%, transforming what had been a four-day process into one that could be completed in just a few hours. This dramatic improvement in deployment velocity delivered substantial cost savings while simultaneously enhancing system stability through the elimination of manual errors. The economic impact of these improvements extended beyond direct cost reduction, enabling more rapid response to business opportunities and accelerating time-to-value for new infrastructure investments.

\ Security and compliance—often challenging aspects of rapid deployment—were strengthened rather than compromised by Aditya’s solution. His implementation automated critical security components including identity management and certificate provisioning, ensuring that accelerated deployments maintained rigorous enterprise security standards. This achievement demonstrated Aditya's comprehensive understanding of the security landscape and his ability to architect solutions that enhance rather than compromise protection measures.

\ For Aditya Mehra personally, the project represented a significant career milestone that showcased his ability to deliver transformative solutions at enterprise scale. The initiative strengthened his expertise across cloud automation, SDDC management, and VMware infrastructure while demonstrating his capacity to lead complex technical initiatives with far-reaching organizational impact. His success in orchestrating complex deployments enhanced his reputation as a technical leader capable of driving large-scale projects and positioned him as a key contributor in the domains of enterprise cloud architecture and automation leadership.

\ This success story illustrates how strategic technical leadership, when combined with innovative automation practices, can fundamentally transform enterprise IT operations. As organizations continue to embrace cloud-native architectures and software-defined infrastructure, Aditya's work serves as a compelling example of how focused leadership can drive exceptional results in enterprise cloud implementation. The methodical approach to automation, coupled with a deep understanding of business requirements, created a solution that delivered value across multiple dimensions of the enterprise technology landscape.

\ Looking ahead, the implications of this project success extend beyond immediate achievements. It demonstrates how effective technical leadership can overcome complex operational challenges while delivering exceptional value to organizations undertaking digital transformation initiatives. The VCF Day 1 orchestration project stands as a model for future infrastructure automation efforts, showcasing the powerful combination of strategic vision, technical excellence, and operational innovation under Aditya Mehra's capable leadership. As the technology landscape continues to evolve, the principles and practices demonstrated in this project provide valuable lessons for organizations seeking to enhance their own infrastructure automation capabilities.

A distinguished technical leader with over 17 years of experience in software development and infrastructure management, Aditya Mehra has established himself as an expert in cloud infrastructure and automation solutions. His comprehensive experience spans both technical innovation and community development, combining deep technical expertise with a passion for social impact. With a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science and Engineering, Aditya has consistently delivered solutions that drive both technical excellence and positive organizational outcomes.

\ His leadership approach emphasizes the integration of cutting-edge technology with practical business value, resulting in implementations that address both immediate needs and long-term strategic objectives. Throughout his career, Aditya has demonstrated exceptional skill in identifying operational inefficiencies and developing innovative technical solutions to address complex business challenges. His ability to bridge the gap between technical possibilities and business requirements has made him a valued advisor and implementation leader across numerous enterprise initiatives.

\ Beyond his technical contributions, Aditya's commitment to education and mentorship has helped develop the next generation of infrastructure and automation specialists. By sharing his knowledge and experience with colleagues and community members, he has created a lasting impact that extends beyond his direct technical contributions. Through his ongoing work across enterprise cloud architecture and automation leadership, Aditya Mehra continues to shape the evolution of infrastructure management practices while fostering a culture of innovation and continuous improvement among technical professionals.

:::tip This story was distributed as a release by Kashvi Pandey under HackerNoon’s Business Blogging Program. Learn more about the program .

items.title: The Bitcoin Faultline: How Jason Voelker’s Case Against Celsius May Redraw the Map of Bankruptcy Law
items.author: Art Stephansonhaxkerson
items.content:
When Celsius Network fell into bankruptcy in 2022, the headlines focused on a multibillion-dollar implosion of a once-thriving crypto lending empire. But underneath the chaos, one case—buried in the docket—has emerged as the most consequential legal battle to arise from the crypto bankruptcy era. It isn’t a class action or a federal indictment. It’s a single pro se litigant: Jason Voelker, from California, standing alone against a system that may be forced to change because of him.

\n Voelker’s case is not about technicalities. It’s about first principles. It raises constitutional questions about judicial integrity, takings without compensation, and the due process owed to creditors in a post-confirmation world. More than that, it exposes the deep incompatibility between traditional bankruptcy doctrine and the rapidly evolving legal architecture of digital asset custody.

\n This is not just a story about 17.61163 bitcoin. It is the front line of a legal transformation. If Voelker succeeds, the implications will be seismic. If he fails, a precedent may be set that allows bankruptcy estates to defeat enforcement by misrepresenting compliance—and forfeiting the truth before anyone can correct the record.

The Spark: A Misrepresentation at the Heart of a Denial

In 2024, Voelker filed a motion for leave to bring an adversary proceeding against Celsius. In that complaint, he alleged that 17 bitcoin were held in bailment under Wyoming’s blockchain custody statutes—assets that were never property of the estate and thus not subject to reorganization.

\ Celsius’s Plan Administrator didn’t contest the allegation. They didn’t argue the 17 bitcoin were part of the estate. Instead, they claimed Voelker was already receiving 105% of his claim under the confirmed Plan and that further relief was barred.

\ The bankruptcy court relied on that assertion. The May 8, 2024 order denied Voelker’s motion on the premise that his 17 bitcoin were already being paid, and he was receiving even more than he asked for.

\ But that wasn’t true. He never received a single coin. And the Plan Administrator knew that when they said otherwise.

A Doctrinal Time Bomb: Judicial Estoppel Meets the Blockchain

This moment is bigger than estoppel. It’s about whether a bankruptcy court, having relied on a factual assertion to deny a litigant access to justice, can allow that same assertion to be quietly abandoned—leaving the creditor without recourse, without remedy, and without rights.

\ It’s about whether a reorganized estate can promise payment to shut the courthouse door—and then walk away, free of obligation, when the dust settles.

\ This is the watershed. Never before has a bankruptcy case so clearly implicated the doctrine of judicial estoppel in the digital asset context. Never before has a plan administrator argued that a creditor is overpaid in order to defeat a lawsuit, then refused to issue the payment and stood silent while a forfeiture order loomed.

Why This Case Will Change the Law

If Voelker’s motion is granted, the law will shift. If it’s denied, the case will become a constitutional time bomb on appeal. Either way, the impact will be felt across bankruptcy courts and digital custodians nationwide.

\ • State Custody Laws Will Be Elevated: Courts will have to reckon with bailment laws like Wyoming’s that deliberately place digital assets outside bankruptcy estates. Voelker’s claim will force that confrontation.

\ • Judicial Estoppel Will Gain Constitutional Dimensions: The consequences of false or strategic representations to bankruptcy courts will no longer be procedural. They will become constitutional violations.

\ • Forfeiture Orders Will Face National Scrutiny: If a court allows forfeiture of assets it previously acknowledged as owed, it will create a takings problem under the Fifth Amendment. The line between abandoned property and confiscated property will be tested like never before.

\ • Post-Confirmation Rights Will Be Redefined: Once a Plan is confirmed, courts typically wash their hands of contested matters. Voelker’s case shows why that assumption is not only outdated—it’s dangerous.

The Clock and the Curtain

The forfeiture deadline is March 31, 2025. On that day, any unclaimed property is slated to be returned to the reorganized entity, Ionic Digital, or used to pay administrative professionals. Voelker’s bitcoin will be gone. His appeal will be mooted. The May 8 order, never enforced, will become a judicial relic—honored in text but dishonored in practice.

\ Voelker has filed an emergency motion asking the court to enforce its own ruling—or at least stay the forfeiture long enough to litigate. The court has ordered responses. The hearing will decide whether bankruptcy courts remain courts of equity, or whether plan finality can override fundamental fairness.

The First Great Test of Crypto Bankruptcy Integrity

This case will be studied for years. It is the first moment where the convergence of blockchain custody, federal bankruptcy law, and constitutional protections have all collided in the same courtroom.

\ Jason Voelker didn’t plan to make history. But by standing firm and forcing the court to reconcile its words with its actions, he has become the plaintiff of record in a legal inflection point.

\ Whether this ends with a victory in bankruptcy court or a groundbreaking decision on appeal, one thing is clear: the old rules are no longer enough. A new era of digital asset insolvency law has begun.

items.title: 75% of Scientists in Nature Poll Weigh Leaving US
items.author: msmash
items.content:
A Nature survey has found that three-quarters of responding U.S. scientists are considering leaving the nation following disruptions to science under the Trump administration. 

Out of 1,608 respondents, 75.3% said they were contemplating leaving the country. Scientists cited concerns over research funding and the general treatment of science as contributing factors for their reasoning. Europe and Canada were mentioned as potential destinations for those looking for opportunities abroad.

items.title: 6 Movie Sequels We Deserve (But Never Got)
items.author: Fayam Ayekame
items.content:
Sequels are a major part of the movie industry with many great movies featuring them. Sometimes, sequels are even teased, leaving room for potential sequels, and leaving audiences wanting more. Unfortunately, things dont always work out and we dont get the sequels, despite audiences desperately needing one. Here are six movies that left audiences wanting more, and have yet to deliver a sequel.

Talk about a failed sci-fi concept, Jumper had all the tools to be one of the biggest movies in the genre. Jumping into the hearts of fans all over the world, Jumper follows as he makes the best of his teleporting abilities. Unfortunately for our hero, the are always watching from the shadows, hunting Jumpers.

\ After several battles including one where proves to be more than an ordinary Jumper, our hero triumphs. With stranded atop a mountain, and the in disarray, there’s enough excitement for a sequel. To top it all off, it turns out ’s mother is a , and she has another kid.

With guns, Explosions, Explosions, and a whole party of violence, The Losers certainly lives up to its action billing. With a star-studded cast, it looked set to be the next big action flick or at least spawn a franchise. Sadly, there haven’t been any indications of a sequel after the first movie.

\ With a villain that survives against all odds, there’s surely more potential for this movie in the future. Fans know isn’t one to take a defeat lying down, meaning he could return with a bang. Plus, there’s the small matter of being responsible for ’s father’s death. Despite a poor performance at the box office, fans are still left wanting more with the way things end.

Aliens hunting other Aliens, with Earth serving as a battlefield, I Am Number Four would thrive in the industry today. The movie follows the children of the nine guardians of the fictional planet . After their parents’ demise, these children are all that stand between the and universal domination. Despite already killing the first three children, six more remain, including number four.

\ After a showdown with the featuring number six’s intro and a badass chimera, the are defeated. We are left with number four and six teaming up and learning the location of the other children. Fans are left wondering how the will respond and how powerful the children will be when united. Let’s hope we get a sequel or a reboot that gives the fans a proper and well-deserved ending.

Before Game of Thrones, was one of the few movies featuring dragons that viewers loved. boasted a C.G.I dragon seemingly ahead of its time, and might even hold up in today’s industry, despite higher standards. With sorcerers, orc-like humans, dragon riders (sort of), and magic, it had everything needed to succeed.

\ Despite ending on a cliffhanger with the reveal of dragon, we never got a sequel taking us back to the beautiful world of . It seems we may never get a sequel nearly 20 years after the film’s original release, despite an abundance of source material. Perhaps a reboot may happen sometime in the future, but fans of are sure to want some closure.

Magical powers with a weird and possibly fatal twist? Yes please. The Covenant revolves around teenagers born into a coven who gain amazing powers. But where there’s power, there’s greed, and this sets the plot for our movie. A play on the distant aftermath of the Salem witch trials, there’s enough mystery to satisfy magic lovers.

\ The Covenant is one of those movies in which the villain seemingly escapes at the end, leaving audiences to wonder, “What next?” Sadly, almost twenty years later, we still have no answers, and none seem forthcoming. If there were ever a sequel or a reboot, many fans would tune in to get the answers they deserve.

James Cameron knows how to create movies that leave fans wanting more, and the same applies to Alita: Battle Angel. We follow as she battles memory loss, and a new relationship, all while trying to fit in. Audiences cant help but love our heroine, and the C.G.I certainly does this movie justice.

\ In the end, we are left with seeking to become the final champion of motorball and earn a place to . We see a nefarious smile from the final boss: , as he watches on with excitement. Sure enough fans would like to know how it’ll go down and what becomes of our beloved battle angel.

items.title: Never Fill Out a PDF Form Again With This Clever Script
items.author: Diana Sihuta
items.content:
Manual PDF form filling remains a tedious and error-prone task in business and administrative workflows, despite the ubiquity of digital tools. This article explores an AI-driven solution to automate the process using Python and large language models (LLMs) like OpenAI’s GPT-4o. By extracting form fields, leveraging contextual AI analysis, and dynamically populating PDFs, the method reduces human intervention to near zero - demonstrated through real-world examples like the IRS W-9 and California’s TR-205 forms. While results vary depending on PDF structure and field clarity, the approach highlights the transformative potential of AI in document automation, offering scalability, speed, and a framework for overcoming challenges like ambiguous field labeling. Discover how even imperfect AI today paves the way for tomorrow’s seamless automation.

This article grew out of my work developing Instafill.ai, a tool designed to streamline PDF form automation for businesses and individuals. While Instafill.ai now handles complex workflows (like legal documents and tax forms), its earliest iteration was a humble Python script - similar to the one I’ll explore here. By sharing this open-source approach, I aim to empower developers to build their own solutions. Think of this as a behind-the-scenes look at how Instafill.ai core technology started - and a roadmap for your experiments.

\ Interactive PDF documents are widely used in business and education, but manually filling them out can be a real challenge. The repetitive nature of this process and the risk of errors during manual data entry often lead to wasted time and frustration. Automation can be a game-changer, significantly reducing document processing time and minimizing errors.

\ My company team aimed to address the pain points of users dealing with PDF forms and we asked ourselves three key questions:

  1. Are there existing products today that solve the problems of manually filling out such forms?
  2. How can we solve these problems, and what additional value would our potential user gain if alternative products already exist?
  3. Can the task of filling PDF forms be entirely delegated to artificial intelligence?

\ To be honest, the last question remains relevant even now. However, the rapid advancement of large language models allows us to confidently bet on reducing human involvement in monotonous PDF form filling to zero. This idea forms the foundation of the solution I will discuss further in this article.

Let’s get to the core. The main concept of the solution involves:

  • Reading the PDF form — extracting text and form fields.
  • Using an artificial intelligence model to analyze and populate the fields.
  • Updating the PDF file with new values.

\ Overall, nothing too complicated, right? This can be implemented using any programming language, but we chose Python specifically because it’s flexible, versatile, and efficient for writing code and conducting experiments. After all, we constantly need to test new hypotheses and make quick decisions based on the results. By the end, you’ll also have a script ready to use on your PDF forms.

Implementation and Step-by-Step Instructions

The script starts by reading the PDF file to extract its text and form fields. The text from the PDF document provides the AI model with general information about the form and its purpose. This sets the context in which the AI model will operate to fill in the required fields.

def extract_pdf_text(pdf_bytes: bytes) -> str:
    pdf_text = ''
    pdf_document = fitz.open(PDF_EXT, pdf_bytes)
    for page_num in range(len(pdf_document)):
        page = pdf_document.load_page(page_num)
        text_page = page.get_textpage()
        pdf_text += text_page.extractText()

    return pdf_text

\ Equally important is extracting as much information as possible about these fields and saving it in a format understandable to the AI model. This improves the quality of the output because the more context and details we provide to the AI, the higher the likelihood of generating the desired result. Large language models tend to always return some output, but the quality can be questionable. Our goal is to minimize the frequency of hallucinations.

def extract_pdf_fields(pdf_bytes: bytes) -> list[dict]:
    form_fields = []
    pdf_document = fitz.open(PDF_EXT, pdf_bytes)

    for page_num in range(len(pdf_document)):
        page = pdf_document.load_page(page_num)
        widget_list = page.widgets()
        if widget_list:
            for widget in widget_list:
                form_fields.append({
                    'name': widget.field_name,
                    'label': widget.field_label,
                    'type': widget.field_type_string,
                    'max_length': widget.text_maxlen
                })

    return form_fields

2. Delegating the Work to AI

Before using the AI to populate the form fields, the script generates a prompt. The quality of this prompt determines whether the "magic" happens. The prompt contains general instructions and accepts PDF components (extracted in the previous step) and unstructured data as parameters. This data is the source of information needed to fill the form. For simplicity, the script reads it from a text file, but in general, it could be any format: PDF, DOCX, JPG, etc.

def fill_fields_prompt(pdf_text: str, fields: list[dict], source_info: str) -> str:
    return f"""
        You are an automated PDF forms filler.
        Your job is to fill the following form fields using the provided materials.
        Field keys will tell you which values they expect:
        {json.dumps(fields)}

        Materials:
        - Text extracted from the PDF form, delimited by <>:
        <{pdf_text}>

        - Source info attached by user, delimited by ##:
        #{source_info}#

        Output a JSON object with key-value pairs where:
        - key is the 'name' of the field,
        - value is the field value you assigned to it.
    """

\ I chose OpenAI and the GPT-4o model as the AI provider. It is sufficiently intelligent and fast. You can experiment with other providers and models, but remember to tailor the prompt for each LLM. Prompt engineering is critical at this stage. After the API call (shown below), we’ll have a dictionary of <field name>:<field value>, ready for the next step.

def call_openai(prompt: str, gpt_model: str = 'gpt-4o'):
    response =  openai_client.chat.completions.create(
        model=gpt_model,
        messages=[{'role': 'system', 'content': prompt}],
        response_format={"type": "json_object"},
        timeout=TIMEOUT,
        temperature=0
    )

    response_data = response.choices[0].message.content.strip()
    return json.loads(response_data)

The final touch is populating the PDF with the values the AI has generated for the form fields.

def fill_pdf_fields(pdf_bytes: bytes, field_values: dict) -> io.BytesIO:
    pdf_document = fitz.open(PDF_EXT, pdf_bytes)
    for page_num in range(len(pdf_document)):
        page = pdf_document.load_page(page_num)
        widget_list = page.widgets()

        if widget_list:
            for widget in widget_list:
                field_name = widget.field_name
                if field_name in field_values:
                    widget.field_value = field_values[field_name]
                    widget.update()

    output_stream = io.BytesIO()
    pdf_document.save(output_stream)
    output_stream.seek(0)

    return output_stream

First and foremost, our product targets a U.S. audience, so as an example, I used the IRS W-9 form — a common document in the United States used to collect taxpayer identification information. This form is often required for freelancing, contractual agreements, and various financial transactions. Given its widespread use, it serves as an ideal example to demonstrate how our solution works.

\ For filling out this form, I used the following test data:

 1. personal info
   john smith
   222 Victory St, 125
   San Francisco, CA, 94111

 2. business info
   BOTMAKERS, LLC
   has foreign partners
   account numbers: 1234567890, 0987654321
   tin: 123456789

 3. requester
   jane smith
   87 Independence St

\ Now for the most interesting part: let’s look at the filled-out form and analyze the quality of the solution’s work.

\ We see that all fields are populated. Ignoring quality for now, our initial conclusion is that the generative AI handled its task. Let’s dig deeper into what it generated: there are clear successes and failures. Some fields (highlighted in green) are filled correctly, while others (highlighted in red) look like someone typed them blindly. Let’s explore why this happened. Here are some of the fields extracted from the PDF form (only a subset is shown; others follow the same pattern):

{
    "name" : "topmostSubform[0].Page1[0].Boxes3a-b_ReadOrder[0].c1_2[0]",
    "label" : null,
    "type" : "CheckBox",
    "max_length" : 0
},
{
    "name" : "topmostSubform[0].Page1[0].f1_05[0]",
    "label" : null,
    "type" : "Text",
    "max_length" : 0
},
{
    "name" : "topmostSubform[0].Page1[0].f1_06[0]",
    "label" : null,
    "type" : "Text",
    "max_length" : 0
},
{
    "name" : "topmostSubform[0].Page1[0].Address_ReadOrder[0].f1_07[0]",
    "label" : null,
    "type" : "Text",
    "max_length" : 0
}

\ The field name is a critical parameter for the AI when filling in values. However, in this case, we face a problem: the names lack sufficient information to unambiguously determine the expected values for these fields. This explains the unsatisfactory results.

\ Let’s try filling out another form — TR-205 (Request for Trial by Written Declaration). This document is used in California to submit a request for a trial by written declaration. It allows drivers to contest traffic violations without appearing in court.

\ I used the following test data to fill it out:

 Court:
 SUPERIOR COURT OF CALIFORNIA
 100 NORTH STATE STREET
 UKIAH CA 95482
 BRANCH #23480

 Citation: KV19133
 Case: 231N07977

 Defendant:
 John Smith
 1234 Main Street, Apt. 101

 Due date: 07/21/24
 Bail amount: $441
 Date mailed: 06/21/24

 Evidence:
  - photographs (5)
  - witness testimony

 Statement of facts: I increased my speed to avoid a possible traffic accident due to a truck in the middle of the road.

 Today: 06/21/24

\ Notice that the script performed better with this form. All provided information landed in the “correct” fields. As you might guess, the difference lies in how these fields are stored in the PDF. Compare them to the previous example:

{
    "name" : "TR-205[0].Page1[0].P1Caption[0].CitationNumber[0].CitationNumber[0]",
    "label" : "CITATION NUMBER:",
    "type" : "Text",
    "max_length" : 0
},
{
    "name" : "TR-205[0].Page1[0].P1Caption[0].CaseNumber[0].CaseNumber[0]",
    "label" : "CASE NUMBER:",
    "type" : "Text",
    "max_length" : 0
}

\ Thus, we discovered that the quality of form filling heavily depends on the internal structure of the input PDF form. How the form fields are stored directly impacts the final result. This remains one of the key challenges I still don’t have a complete solution for.

Let’s summarize. In this article, we explored how AI can automate filling PDF forms, using the IRS W-9 and TR-205 as examples. The core idea was to demonstrate how manual labor and errors can be reduced by automating document processing. All materials used in this article, including the original Python code, are available on GitHub.

\ Feel free to leave comments, share your thoughts, or discuss your own experiences. If you have questions or want to explore any aspects of this article further, reach out to me!

items.title: Sushira Transforms Corporate Mentorship Through Innovative Technology-Driven Program
items.author: Kashvi Pandey
items.content:
\ In an era where employee development and retention pose critical challenges for organizations, Sushira Somavarapu's groundbreaking mentorship program at a leading tech company stands as a testament to the transformative power of technology-driven solutions. By combining behavioral science with advanced automation, this initiative has redefined how large organizations approach mentorship and professional development, setting new standards for scalability and effectiveness in corporate learning initiatives.

\ Under visionary leadership, the program harnessed a workflow automation platform to create a comprehensive, data-driven mentorship framework that reshaped the landscape of professional development. Its technological foundation marked a significant departure from traditional approaches, incorporating behavioral science principles and nudging techniques to foster meaningful relationships and achieve measurable outcomes. This innovative model not only transformed the mentorship experience but also created a scalable framework for future organizational development.

\ At the heart of this program was the transformation of the mentor-mentee matching process. What previously required a week of intensive manual effort was reimagined as an instantaneous, automated system. By analyzing and optimizing inefficiencies in the matching algorithm, the program introduced an advanced optimization model that improved average match scores by 13 percentage points and increased match rates by 24 percentage points. This breakthrough ensured participants received high-quality pairings, fundamentally enhancing the program’s effectiveness and participant satisfaction.

\ The integration of a messaging platform added another layer of innovation, enabling the automated delivery of weekly discussion prompts and resources directly to participants. This streamlined communication ensured consistent engagement between mentors and mentees, while the automation guaranteed timely and relevant content delivery throughout the mentorship journey. These improvements significantly boosted user experience and sustained participation levels.

\ Beyond technological advancements, the program achieved exceptional results in addressing critical organizational challenges. Career advancement metrics highlighted its impact, with a 21.6 percentage point increase in promotion rates, raising success rates from 44.4% to 66.0%. Similarly, participant groups saw a 6.65% reduction in attrition over 12 months, translating to substantial cost savings, from reduced turnover. By scaling the program across regions, it reached over 10,000 employees per quarter starting in August 2021, showcasing its scalability and reach.

\ The financial benefits extended beyond retention. Automation efforts led to significant operational efficiencies, saving the equivalent of eight full-time positions annually. These outcomes, combined with improved promotion and retention metrics, established the program as a benchmark for technology-enabled mentorship initiatives, delivering a compelling return on investment.

\ This innovative approach to program design exemplified how cutting-edge technology can address complex organizational challenges. Through ongoing refinements based on participant feedback and performance metrics, the program remained dynamic and adaptive to changing needs, ensuring high standards of quality and effectiveness.

\ The success of the program has established new benchmarks for mentorship initiatives in large organizations. Its scalable framework enables other organizations to develop skilled workforces while achieving significant economic benefits. By combining data-driven decision-making with behavioral science, the program offers a model that can be adapted to drive success in diverse organizational contexts.

\ Looking ahead, the implications of this program extend far beyond its initial achievements. It demonstrates that innovative technology solutions, when thoughtfully applied to workforce development challenges, can deliver exceptional results at scale. As organizations continue evolving their professional development strategies in a digital-first world, this initiative stands as a model for future programs, showcasing the power of behavioral science, automation, and analytics in advancing organizational excellence.

\ Through this transformative program, a new standard for mentorship has been set, proving that with the right combination of technology, science, and innovation, organizations can create scalable solutions that deliver meaningful results for both individuals and the business as a whole.

\ A distinguished innovator and accomplished data scientist, Sushira Somavarapu has established herself as a leading expert at the intersection of technology, behavioral science, and workforce development. Armed with a Master’s degree in Analytics from Louisiana State University and professional certifications such as SAS Base Programmer, she combines technical precision with strategic vision. Her expertise spans technological innovation, machine learning, predictive analytics, and business intelligence, consistently delivering solutions that merge technical sophistication with practical value.

\ Throughout her career, she has excelled in optimizing workflows and implementing data-driven methodologies that redefine employee development strategies. Her expertise in automation and human development principles has consistently driven operational excellence while maintaining high participant engagement. Recognized as an innovator and leader in the field of data science, she continues to drive transformative change and shape the future of AI-driven workforce development.

:::tip This story was distributed as a release by Kashvi Pandey under HackerNoon’s Business Blogging Program. Learn more about the program .

items.title: This Open-source Tool Lets You Build Your Own Financial Manager Using AI
items.author: Ekemini Samuel
items.content:
In this tutorial, we’ll walk you through a step-by-step process of building an AI-powered Investment & Savings Copilot that helps you plan your savings and investments using , Nextjs, and CopilotKit🪁.

\ Here’s what we’ll cover:

  • Building up a Next.js project for the AI Investment & Savings Copilot.

  • Adding CopilotKit UI components and API endpoints to your app.

  • Integrating Maybe Finance API to provide personalized investment and savings insights.

Here’s a preview of the AI-Powered Finance App implementation:

This lets you define your copilot’s role without dealing with complex setups or integrations.

 is the open-source OS for personal finance, providing tools to securely manage, plan, and optimize your savings and investments.

\ In our project, we’ll tap into Maybe Finance’s real-time data and analytics to power our copilot’s insights—helping users optimize their savings and investments.

Before we begin, make sure you have:

  • Intermediate understanding of Next.js and React

  • Basic knowledge of TypeScript

  • Code editor (VS Code recommended)

Once you get this sorted, we’ll start development! 🚀

\ This is the web application we will be building.

1. Create a New Next.js Project

Open your terminal and run this command to create a new Next.js project

npx create-next-app@latest finance_ai --typescript

\ Navigate to the project directory and start the development server:

cd finance_ai
npm run dev

Next, let’s install the necessary dependencies for this project. These include:

  • Axios ( to fetch the API)

  • Shadcn/ui (for pre-built UI components)

  • Recharts (for chart representation)

Run the following command to install them:

npm install @copilotkit/react-ui @copilotkit/react-core axios

\ Then, for Shadcn UI, run this command

npx shadcn@latest add button card input label badge tabs

Note: If you are using CopilotKit Cloud, you don’t need to include the runtime package, as the CopilotKit Cloud automatically manages the runtime environment. However, if you are self-hosting the backend, you may need to handle runtime configurations manually. In this tutorial, we will focus on self-hosting, but keep in mind that CopilotKit Cloud is an option that simplifies runtime management.

\ Now, let’s set up the project structure. The key files and directories we’ll create include:

  • src/app/ui/service/index.ts – Handles API calls to fetch financial data.

  • src/app/ui/components/FinanceCopilot.tsx – The main UI component for our AI-powered financial assistant.

  • src/app/lib/types/investment.ts – Defines TypeScript types for investment and savings data.

  • src/app/lib/data/mockData.ts – Contains financial data for testing.

  • src/app/api/copilotkit/route.ts – The API endpoint for CopilotKit.

  • src/app/api/finance/route.ts – An API for fetching financial insights.

Below is the file tree for our application:

Building the Finance AI Frontend

1. Setting Up Global Providers in 

First, go to  to obtain your CopilotKit Cloud key for authentication and API access. You can sign up with GitHub or Google.

Then, copy your API key and save it in your  file. Then, update your global layout to wrap your app with the CopilotKit provider. This makes the copilot functionality available throughout your application.

Now that it is set, enter the code below in this directory finance_ai/app/layout.tsx

import { Metadata } from "next";
import { Geist, Geist_Mono } from "next/font/google";
import "./globals.css";
import { CopilotKit } from "@copilotkit/react-core";
import "@copilotkit/react-ui/styles.css";

// Load fonts for styling
const geistSans = Geist({
  variable: "--font-geist-sans",
  subsets: ["latin"],
});
const geistMono = Geist_Mono({
  variable: "--font-geist-mono",
  subsets: ["latin"],
});

// Metadata for SEO and display
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: "Coyamin - AI Investment Copilot",
  description: "Make informed financial decisions with AI-powered insights.",
};

// Retrieve the Copilot API key from environment variables
const copilotApiKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COPILOTKIT_API_KEY;

export default function RootLayout({
  children,
}: Readonly<{ children: React.ReactNode }>) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <body className={`${geistSans.variable} ${geistMono.variable} antialiased`}>
        {/* Conditionally wrap with CopilotKit if the API key is available */}
        {copilotApiKey ? (
          <CopilotKit publicApiKey={copilotApiKey}>{children}</CopilotKit>
        ) : (
          <>{children}</>
        )}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

\ In the code above, we start by importing global styles and fonts, ensuring a consistent design throughout the application.

\ Next, we wrap the app with the  provider to use the API key; <CopilotKitProvider apiKey={process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_COPILOTKIT_API_KEY}><App /> </CopilotKitProvider>

\ This setup makes AI functionality accessible across all components in the application.

2. Creating the Login and Onboarding Screens

To create a login form component, enter this code in app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts:

import NextAuth from "next-auth";
import GoogleProvider from "next-auth/providers/google";
import { PrismaAdapter } from "@auth/prisma-adapter";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/db";

export const authOptions = {
  adapter: PrismaAdapter(prisma),
  providers: [
    GoogleProvider({
      clientId: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID as string,
      clientSecret: process.env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET as string,
    }),
  ],
  callbacks: {
    async session({ session, user }) {
      if (session?.user) {
        session.user.id = user.id;
        session.user.isOnboarded = user.isOnboarded;
      }
      return session;
    },
  },
  pages: {
    signIn: "/",
  },
};

const handler = NextAuth(authOptions);
export { handler as GET, handler as POST };

\ The authentication page is split into two tabs: Login and Sign Up. Each form uses labeled input fields and a button that redirects to the onboarding page upon successful authentication.

```

 ![](https://cdn.hackernoon.com/images/6ueXhxLrWwbOXyBsdC6YryiZQeF2-vd93c3z.png)

### The Onboarding Page

The onboarding page has a **chat interface** that asks questions such as financial goals, risk tolerance, and investment preferences as part of the onboarding process.

\
In the `app/onboarding` directory, create a `page.tsx` file and enter this code:

\

import { useState, useEffect } from "react" import { useRouter } from "next/navigation" import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button" import { Card, CardContent } from "@/components/ui/card" import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge" import { ArrowRight, Bot, User, Sparkles } from "lucide-react"

interface Message { type: "bot" | "user" content: string options?: string[] }

export default function OnboardingChat() { const router = useRouter() const [messages, setMessages] = useState

\
Here, we import the Shadcn UI button, card, and badge components and also add state management with `import { useState, useEffect } from "react".` We also include an `OnboardingChat()` function with a message to welcome the user and ask about their financial goals, which they can select from the options as shown below:

__[ \n ](https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Foxs8m960tl312dg79d7g.png)__    ![](https://cdn.hackernoon.com/images/6ueXhxLrWwbOXyBsdC6YryiZQeF2-cta3c15.png)

**Handling User Selection and Chat Flow**

When you select an option, the chatbot responds dynamically and progresses through different stages of onboarding. We also include a dropdown that explains each risk tolerance level:

\

typescript // Define the steps of the onboarding process const steps = [ { title: "Financial Goals", progress: 33 }, { title: "Risk Tolerance", progress: 66 }, { title: "Investment Preferences", progress: 100 }, ];

const handleOptionSelect = (option: string) => { setMessages((prev) => […prev, { type: "user", content: option }]); setSelectedOptions((prev) => ({ …prev, [currentStep]: […(prev[currentStep] || []), option], }));

setTimeout(() => {
  if (currentStep === 0) {
    setMessages((prev) => [
      ...prev,
      {
        type: "bot",
        content: "Great choice! Now, what's your risk tolerance level?",
        options: ["Conservative (Low Risk)", "Moderate (Medium Risk)", "Aggressive (High Risk)"],
      },
    ]);
    setCurrentStep(1);
  } else if (currentStep === 1) {
    setMessages((prev) => [
      ...prev,
      {
        type: "bot",
        content: "Perfect! Finally, which investment types interest you? (You can select multiple)",
        options: ["Stocks", "ETFs", "Bonds", "Crypto", "Real Estate", "Mutual Funds"],
      },
    ]);
    setCurrentStep(2);
  } else if (currentStep === 2) {
    setMessages((prev) => [
      ...prev,
      {
        type: "bot",
        content: "Thanks! I'll now create your personalized investment profile based on your preferences.",
      },
    ]);
    setTimeout(() => {
      router.push("/dashboard");
    }, 1500);
  }
}, 500);

\
### 3. Creating the Dashboard

The dashboard displays the financial overview and AI-generated recommendations. Enter the code snippets below in `app/dashboard/page.tsx`

\
Integrating the dashboard wrapper with the CopilotKit Sidebar 

\
This wraps the entire dashboard with the`CopilotSidebar` component and sets up the main flex container for the dashboard content.

\

import { CopilotSidebar } from "@copilotkit/react-ui";

export default function DashboardPage() { return (

Dashboard content goes here */}
); }
\
The `CopilotSidebar` provides a persistent AI chat interface, while the flex container (`div` with `flex h-[calc(100vh-3.5rem)]`) ensures the dashboard occupies the full height of the viewport minus a header offset.

\
Also, the inner container ( `<div className="flex-1 p-4 sm:p-6 lg:p-8 overflow-auto">`) is padded and scrollable, ready to hold our overview and chart components.

\
**Implementing the Overview cards in the dashboard**

\
The “Overview” section of the dashboard defines a grid of cards, each displaying a key financial metric such as **Total Balance, Investments, Monthly Savings, and Risk Score**.

\
Still in the `page.tsx` file, enter the code below:

\

\
Each `Card` is composed of a header (`CardHeader` and `CardTitle`) for the label and content (`CardContent`) for the metric value and description. The grid layout (`grid gap-4 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4`) is to make sure that the cards are evenly spaced and responsive.

\
Adding the charts with Rechart 

\
We use Recharts to create two types of charts in the application:**a line chart for the portfolio overview and a pie chart for asset allocation**. These charts are to visualize the financial trends and asset distribution clearly. For more details about Recharts, check out the __[Recharts documentation](https://recharts.org/en-US/)__.

\
Run this command to install Recharts:

javascript npm install recharts

\
And then add this code to the `page.tsx` file in the dashboard directory:

\

typescript import { LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer, PieChart, Pie, Cell, } from "recharts";

const chartData = [ { name: "Jan", value: 4000 }, { name: "Feb", value: 3000 }, { name: "Mar", value: 2000 }, { name: "Apr", value: 2780 }, { name: "May", value: 1890 }, { name: "Jun", value: 2390 }, ];

const pieData = [ { name: "Stocks", value: 400 }, { name: "Bonds", value: 300 }, { name: "Real Estate", value: 300 }, { name: "Crypto", value: 200 }, ];

const COLORS = ["#0088FE", "#00C49F", "#FFBB28", "#FF8042"];

export default function ChartsSection() { return (

{/* Portfolio Overview - Line Chart */} 
  {/* Asset Allocation - Pie Chart */}
  <div className="col-span-1 md:col-span-2 lg:col-span-3">
    <div className="border rounded shadow p-4">
      <h3 className="text-lg font-semibold mb-2">Asset Allocation</h3>
      <ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height="250px">
        <PieChart>
          <Pie data={pieData} innerRadius={50} outerRadius={80} paddingAngle={5} dataKey="value">
            {pieData.map((entry, index) => (
              <Cell key={`cell-${index}`} fill={COLORS[index % COLORS.length]} />
            ))}
          </Pie>
          <Tooltip />
        </PieChart>
      </ResponsiveContainer>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

\
In the code above, the **Line Chart (Portfolio Overview)** uses a `ResponsiveContainer` to ensure the chart is fully responsive. The `LineChart` component plots the data with a `CartesianGrid` for visual reference, while the `XAxis` and `YAxis` provide labels for readability. Using Recharts in this modular way keeps the code clear and maintainable.

\
Then, update `pages/index.tsx` to display the Dashboard in the main application.

\

javascript import Dashboard from '../components/Dashboard';

export default function Home() { return (

); }
\
 ![](https://cdn.hackernoon.com/images/6ueXhxLrWwbOXyBsdC6YryiZQeF2-jbb3cpx.png)

### 4. Setting Up the Backend Endpoints

**CopilotKit Endpoint**

In `pages/api/copilotkit.ts`, set up the backend endpoint to process requests from the Copilot UI. This endpoint will forward requests to our AI engine (and interface with Maybe Finance):

\

javascript import { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from 'next'; import { copilotRuntimeNextJSAppRouterEndpoint } from '@copilotkit/runtime';

\
### **Maybe Finance API Endpoint**

Now, let's integrate Maybe Finance with our Coyamin application. We'll create several API routes that act as secure intermediaries between our frontend and the Maybe Finance API.

\
First, set up environment variables to store our API credentials:

\

javascript APImaybeapiURL=https://api.synthfinance.com

\
 ![](https://cdn.hackernoon.com/images/6ueXhxLrWwbOXyBsdC6YryiZQeF2-ftc3cmd.png)

We will create a series of API routes that will serve as our financial data pipeline. Each route will handle a specific type of financial data:

\
Currency Information API

\
This fetches different currencies for any information needed during the chat. Create a`route.ts` file in the `app/api/rates/live` directory:

\

javascript import { NextResponse } from "next/server"; import axios from "axios";

const APIKEY; const BASEURL;

export async function GET(req: Request) { try { const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url); const to = searchParams.get("to");

if (!to) {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { message: "Missing required query parameter: to" },
    { status: 400 }
  );
}

const response = await axios.get(`${BASE_URL}/rates/live`, {
  params: { to },
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${API_KEY}`,
    Accept: "application/json",
  },
});

return NextResponse.json(response.data, { status: 200 });

} catch (error: unknown) { if(error instanceof Error) { return NextResponse.json( { message: "Error fetching live rates", error: error.message }, { status: 500 } ); } return NextResponse.json( { message: "Error fetching live rates"}, { status: 500 } ); } }

\
This endpoint allows you to see how currency values and investment opportunities have changed over time to make data-driven decisions.

## 5. Running the Application

Start your development server:

\

\
Open __[http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000/)__ in your browser. You should see the dashboard with the Copilot sidebar. Log in, complete the onboarding chat, and then interact with the copilot to get financial insights powered by CopilotKit and Maybe Finance.

\
> Sign Up Page

\
 ![](https://cdn.hackernoon.com/images/6ueXhxLrWwbOXyBsdC6YryiZQeF2-txd3ck1.png)

\
> Onboarding

\
 ![](https://cdn.hackernoon.com/images/6ueXhxLrWwbOXyBsdC6YryiZQeF2-vxe3c1p.png)

\
The result is a fully functioning AI-Powered Finance App using CopilotKit:

[https://youtu.be/eOg29zBpuM4?si=SpyICTkqVkx05sC-&embedable=true](https://youtu.be/eOg29zBpuM4?si=SpyICTkqVkx05sC-&embedable=true)

\
To clone the project from __[GitHub](https://github.com/Tabintel/finance_ai)__and run it locally, open your terminal and run this command: \n

javascript git clone https://github.com/Tabintel/finance_ai.git ```

\n Then run  to install all dependencies needed for the project and  to run the web app.

In this guide, we walked through the process of building an AI-powered Investment & Savings Copilot using  and  to help users plan their finances efficiently.

\ While we’ve covered key features, this is just the beginning—CopilotKit enables countless AI-driven use cases, from personalized financial assistants to intelligent automation in fintech applications.

\ Check out the full source code on GitHub 

\ Follow CopilotKit on  and say hi, and if you'd like to build something cool, join the  community.

items.title: Microsoft President Calls For a National Talent Strategy For Electricians
items.author: msmash
items.content:
theodp writes: "As I prepared for a White House meeting last fall on the nation's electricity needs," begins Microsoft President Brad Smith in The Country Needs More Electricity --And More Electricians, a Fox Business op-ed. "I met with the leaders at Microsoft who are building our AI infrastructure across the country. During our discussion, I asked them to identify the single biggest challenge for data center expansion in the U.S. I expected they would mention slow permitting, delays in bringing more power online or supply chain constraints -- all significant challenges. But instead, they highlighted a national shortage of people. Electricians, to be precise." 

Much as Smith has done in the past as he declared crisis-level shortages of Computer Science, cybersecurity, and AI talent, he's calling for the nation's politicians and educators to step up to the plate and deliver students trained to address the data center expansion plans of Microsoft and Big Tech.

"How many new electricians must the U.S. recruit and train over the next decade?" Smith asks. "Probably half a million. [...] The good news is that these are good jobs. The bad news is that we don't have a national strategy to recruit and train the people to fill these jobs. Given the Trump administration's commitment to supporting American workers, American jobs and American innovation, we believe that recruiting and training more electricians should rise to its list of priorities. There are several ways to address this issue, and they deserve consideration. For example, we need to do more as a nation to revitalize the industrial arts and shop classes in American high schools. [...] This should be a priority for local school boards, state governors and appropriate federal support. [..] We must also adopt a broad perspective on where new technology is taking us. The tech sector is most often focused on computer and data science -- people who code. But the future will also be built in critical ways by a new generation of engineers, electricians, plumbers, pipefitters, iron workers, carpenters and other skilled trades. So, is 'Learn to Wire' the new 'Learn to Code'?

items.title: This Tool Unlocks Unlimited Free Data for Testing, Prototyping, and Demos
items.content:
tool can generate random mock customised data for free. No limit on number of records that can be generated.

\ Mock random data can be used for variety of purpose such as showing realistic data to customers, performance testing, data analytics, for fun etc.

  1. No limit on data generation
  2. AI integrated to generate attribute data
  3. Javascript dynamic generation of attribute value
  4. Multiple export format supported — JSON, EXCEL, CSV, XML, HTML
  5. Flat and Nested data support
  6. Support existing json schema and json data upload
  7. Around 1.5K pre-defined data keys to choose
  8. User define data list supported
  9. No backend storage and data sharing

\ Tool provide intuitive UI to create and generate plain as well as nested data. Generation screen is divided into two parts left pane is used to define data structure which provides a tree like structure that is well suited for hierarchical as well as flat data. Right pane shows preview of the data that will be generated.

\ Data export format supported :

Let’s quickly jump over to steps required to generate the data with our own customisation.

To generate data user has to define attributes(columns in CSV), each attribute consist of following -

  1. Name of Attribute (Column name) : Specify required attribute name for data field. In case of JSON this corresponds to json field name and in case of CSV format attribute name corresponds to column name.

  2. Value of Attribute (Column value) : Attribute value is randomly set from list of values.— name, country code, book title, actors etc.

    Attribute values can be picked - a. Based on pre defined list of around 1.5 K categories b. AI based generated list c. User defined list d. Generate through javascript program

Data type define the type of generated data for attribute or column. For most people data type is sufficient for data production.

Other data type give granular control for generation of data for JSON format.

Different data types supported : : Sufficient for most use cases. Example — “West Cherry”, “Law”, “1”, “2”, “West Cherry 1.2”, “true”, “false” etc. : Number data type is specifically used for generation of any number which include integer, decimal etc. Example — 1, 2, 2.6, -2 etc. : Boolean data type is used to define either true or false value. : Object data type is used for defining nested attribute (attribute that contains child attributes).  Array data type is used to define list of values. Example — [“West Cherry”, “Law”]

Generation screen provide few controls button that help in defining attributes such as add, delete, edit and more button.

Add — Add button is used to add child attribute Edit — Edit button is used to edit selected attribute Delete — Delete selected attribute Save — Save currently edited attribute More — More button provide extra configuration based on data type

\ Once data structure is defined and preview of sample data generated in preview screen looks good, simply provide number of records to be generated and data output format in the bottom left of screen.

Finally click on  button to generate data based on selected provided attributes.

Let’s quickly go through all the step to generate basic 2 column CSV.

Generate CSV with 2 columns — name and age

  1. name : characters data type

  • Add 2 attributes to data definition screen. For first attribute set attribute name as and for second attribute set name as

  • Select value for both the attributes by clicking on value selection box

  • Selection box list pre-defined values to choose from list of thousand of attributes. For this example let’s choose 

  • For age attribute let’s select define custom values. To create custom values click on more icon. Add minimum and maximum value, in case of age let’s select minimum as 10 and maximum as 80 to set random value for age between 10 and 80.

  • Preview screen will show preview of 10 records

  • Now it’s time to generate the data. Select number of rows to be generated and data format as CSV. Click on Generate button to generate csv data.

  • CSV data is downloaded to the Downloads folder.

Customize as per your wish. Bloomer mock also provide advanced feature such AI generated list, user defined custom list and if you are a developer you can even use javascript to generate and modify output.

\ I will create separate article on advanced features around generation and other capabilities.

\ Feel free to provide your input, suggestions and feedback in the comment section.

\ Enjoy, hope this tool help you in your data generation needs. Have fun generating data! 🚀

items.title: Want to Boost Your Sales? AI Knows Exactly How to Speak to Every Generation
items.author: MTP Business Boost
items.content:
Welcome to MTP Business Boost — a series of expert publications filled with real-world strategies, transforming complex ideas into simple, actionable advice you can apply immediately (MTP stands for Marketing, Technology, and Psychology).

\ In just 9 minutes, you’ll uncover how AI isn’t just adapting marketing communication for different generations—it’s giving businesses the power to dominate. By leveraging AI-driven personalization and precision, you can craft messages that resonate deeply, drive higher engagement, maximize conversions, and secure long-term customer loyalty. The result? Reduced costs, a stronger competitive edge, and undeniable market leadership.

\ Personalization isn’t just a trend—it’s an expectation. A McKinsey study found that 71% of consumers expect personalized interactions, and 76% feel frustrated when companies fail to deliver them. This frustration often leads to lost sales and weakened brand loyalty. On the flip side, companies that embrace personalization can see revenue increases of 5-15% and a boost in marketing ROI by 10-30%. Without it, businesses risk inefficiencies in customer acquisition and retention, ultimately falling behind their competitors ().

\ Before you dive in, make sure you’re subscribed so you don’t miss the next expert insights packed with practical recommendations. Let’s get started!

Weekend on a Yacht and Unexpected Consequences

The golden hues of the Sydney sunrise painted the glass towers in warm amber as Alex leaned back in his sleek office chair, staring at the analytics report on his screen. His IT startup, a promising SaaS company specializing in cybersecurity solutions, had been steadily growing. But now, the data revealed an unexpected challenge—his customer base spanned multiple generations, each responding differently to marketing messages. What worked for Gen Z seemed irrelevant to Boomers, and Millennials had their own distinct preferences.

\ Frustration crept in. How could he fine-tune his communication without hiring a team of specialists or overhauling his entire strategy? The budget was tight, the stakes were high, and he couldn’t afford to misstep.

\ Just then, his phone buzzed. It was Daniel, an old friend and fellow entrepreneur, but leagues ahead in the game—seasoned, successful, and always three steps ahead. "Mate, your voice sounds tense. You need to unwind. Come spend the weekend on my yacht. A little time away will do you good."

\ Alex hesitated. Leaving work for an entire weekend? Now? But something about Daniel’s tone made him reconsider. And so, with a mix of reluctance and curiosity, Alex accepted.

\ The Sydney Harbour shimmered under the midday sun, a dazzling contrast to the deep blue waters. As Alex stepped onto Daniel’s sleek, state-of-the-art yacht—a floating masterpiece of luxury—he felt an unexpected sense of release. Polished teak, crisp ocean air, and the gentle lapping of waves made the city’s worries feel miles away.

\ Over gourmet seafood and a chilled bottle of vintage Chardonnay, Alex shared his predicament. Daniel listened, nodding knowingly. "You’re making it harder than it needs to be," he finally said. "AI can handle this in minutes. It can analyze generational preferences, tailor messaging to their values and interests, and ensure each group receives communication that truly resonates."

\ Alex raised an eyebrow. AI? Sure, his company used machine learning for cybersecurity, but for marketing? Daniel smirked, pulled out his tablet, and with a few taps, demonstrated how AI could segment audiences, adapt messaging for different generations, and align content with their unique expectations and behaviors.

\ The next morning, back in his office, Alex hesitated for only a second before trying it himself. Within minutes, AI had analyzed his customer segments, optimized messaging for each generation, and provided actionable insights. It was effortless. It was powerful. And it worked.

As he leaned back, a satisfied grin spread across his face. This wasn’t just a solution—it was a revelation. For the first time in weeks, he felt in control, ahead of the curve.

\ And to think, it all started with a simple phone call and a weekend on a yacht.

Winning Every Generation: AI-Powered Messaging Guide

Let’s take an example and see how to adapt a marketing message for different generations. We’ll use a sample ad from a hypothetical SaaS company offering a sales automation product. Here’s the original marketing message:

\ Boost your sales like never before with the ultimate SaaS solution for automating your pipeline! Say goodbye to manual follow-ups and hello to smart workflows that save you time and close deals faster. With AI-powered insights, you’ll know exactly when to reach out to prospects for maximum impact. Try it today and watch your revenue soar in just weeks. Sign up for a free 14-day trial – no credit card required!

\ I recommend taking your own marketing message, following the steps below, and applying them right away. This way, you’ll put the knowledge into practice and see results quickly.

2. Paste the following instruction for the AI and press Enter:

\ Hello. You are a world-class expert in effective marketing communications. I need help adapting the following advertising message for optimal reception by my audience, which consists of the Baby Boomer generation.

Boost your sales like never before with the ultimate SaaS solution for automating your pipeline! Say goodbye to manual follow-ups and hello to smart workflows that save you time and close deals faster. With AI-powered insights, you’ll know exactly when to reach out to prospects for maximum impact. Try it today and watch your revenue soar in just weeks. Sign up for a free 14-day trial – no credit card required!

\ Instead of “Baby Boomer,” you can swap in the generation your customers belong to:

  • The Silent Generation (born 1928–1945)

  • Millennials, Gen Y (born 1981–1996)

  • Zoomers, Gen Z (born 1997–2012)

  • Gen Alpha (born 2013–2025)

Here’s how the AI adapted the message for the Baby Boomer generation:

\ 3. You can also ask the AI to explain why the adapted message works for that generation. Use this instruction:

\ Write a detailed argument explaining why this advertising text is suitable for the generation of my clients.

\ Here’s how the AI explained it in our example:

\ 4. Now, let’s ask the AI to adapt the message for another generation, like Gen Y (born 1981–1996):

\ Now, adapt this advertising message for Generation Y and explain why it is effective specifically for this generation.

\ The AI will also explain why the message fits Gen Y.

\ \ 5. You can also have the AI adapt the message for all generations at once to compare them:

\ Adapt this advertising message for all generations and present the results in a table format

\ 6. Plus, you can get the table as an Excel file:

\ Generate this table in Excel format

\ Besides Excel, the AI can provide the results in CSV or PDF formats.

\ 7. You can also ask the AI for specific tips on communicating with your customers’ generation. In our example, try this instruction:

\ Write the most effective recommendations for communication with the Baby Boomer generation. My product is a sales automation service

\ 8. To make your communication with clients as effective as possible and avoid missteps, ask the AI:

\ What mistakes should be avoided in communication with the Baby Boomer generation and why?

\ 9. After getting a lot of information from the AI, you can ask it to summarize everything into a handy PDF:

\ Summarize all the information in this dialogue and present it in PDF format

\ Here’s what the AI-generated PDF might include:

With AI, you can adapt almost any type of marketing communication: emails, Google Ads, social media posts, sales proposals, landing page content, and more.

\ If you have data on your customers’ ages, you can group them by generation and make your communication even more personal and targeted. For example, if your email list has 100,000 subscribers—30,000 from Gen X and 70,000 from Gen Y—a single generic email might not work as well as two versions tailored to each generation. Now you know how to do this with AI, and it only takes a few minutes!

\ Test the results to see the impact of adapting your marketing for specific generations. For instance, when sending an email, create two versions and check which one gets better conversions.

In this article, you’ve learned how to easily and quickly adapt your communication with clients based on the psychological perception of the generation they belong to.

\ If the knowledge you gained from this article has been valuable and helpful to you, you can show your appreciation to the author by making a donation via or giving a like and sharing it.

\ Would you also like to learn more about how AI can be used to grow your business and increase your market share? Feel free to leave your questions in the comments!

Gene Misiev is a marketing expert with over seven years of experience in digital marketing. He thrives in highly competitive "red ocean" B2B markets. His focus is on growth marketing, market research, and strategic development for a complex SaaS product—a tender platform for government and private procurement. He has completed advanced training in Strategic Marketing and Generative AI applications in business at the Institute of Applied Technology (Australia). He has also undertaken specialized courses in leveraging ChatGPT for business and marketing automation.

See You in the Next Issue

Thanks for reading! Make sure you’re subscribed so you don’t miss the next publication, which will bring even more insights to help you expand your business empire with the power of marketing, technology and psychology.

items.title: Why I Stopped Overthinking and Shipped My CSS Cheat Sheet
items.author: tekisolves
items.content:
Perfection kills momentum.

\ I’ve spent months building Teki Solves — reading, coding, refining… and honestly, overthinking way too much.Write, delete, plan, second-guess. Sound familiar?

\ Last week, I finally got tired of waiting.

\ No grand launch. No pixel-perfect design. I just shipped it.

\ Fast forward: — all organic, no ads, just real people finding value in something simple and honest.

\ What I learned (again):

  • People want helpful, not flawless.
  • Momentum only happens in public.

\ I’m sharing this because I know some of you are sitting on great ideas, tools, or blog posts you ship — but haven’t.Stop waiting. Hit publish. See what happens.

\ The faster you put it out, the faster you’ll learn, and the more confidence you’ll build.

\ If I had waited until it was “perfect,” it wouldn’t exist — and I wouldn’t have connected with 84 people this week. That’s 84 little reminders that shipping messy is better than never shipping at all.

\ And if you’re curious about the journey, I’m building everything in the open at Teki Solves.

\ Let’s keep it simple. Problem solved.
